#This is the pipeline for data analysis for barley germination experiment 4

#Load packages
```{r}
BiocManager::install("cleave")
BiocManager::install("Mfuzz")
BiocManager::install("Biobase")
BiocManager::install("org.At.tair.db")
BiocManager::install("clusterProfiler")

library(AnnotationDbi)
library(org.At.tair.db)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggbreak)
library(cowplot)
library(stringr)
library(kableExtra)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(mice)
library(reshape2)
library(data.table)
library(cleaver)
library(Mfuzz)
library(Biobase)
library(textshape)
library(tidyr)
library(gridExtra)
library(grid)
library(pcaMethods)
library(impute)
library(pheatmap)
library(ggh4x)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(ape)



```
#Calculate the digestion efficiency. This is directly connected with the goodness of protein identification. 

```{r}
#Load evidence (TD) file. 

evidence_Hv4_b1 <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/MQ_evs/evidence_Hv4_b1.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t")
evidence_Hv4_b2 <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/MQ_evs/evidence_Hv4_b2.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t")
evidence_Hv4_b3 <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/MQ_evs/evidence_Hv4_b3.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t")
evidence_Hv4_b4 <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/MQ_evs/evidence_Hv4_b4.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t")
evidence_Hv4_b5 <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/MQ_evs/evidence_Hv4_b5.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

#Generate a bar plot from the column missed cleavages
# Batch 1
Try_Dig_b1 <- ggplot(evidence_Hv4_b1, aes(x = factor(Missed.cleavages))) +
  geom_bar(fill = "#105CB6", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Tryptic digestion efficiency (Hv4_batch1)",
       x = "Missed Cleavages",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, family = "", face = "plain"),  
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12, family = "", face = "plain"),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 12, family = "", face = "plain")) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 10)),
        axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 8)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 15)))
ggsave("../../../R_export/Hv4/trypdig/Hv4_b1_trypdig.png")

# Batch 2
Try_Dig_b2 <- ggplot(evidence_Hv4_b2, aes(x = factor(Missed.cleavages))) +

# Load the patchwork package
library(patchwork)

# Combine all 20 plots into a single object
combined_plot <- Try_Dig_b1 + Try_Dig_b2 + Try_Dig_b3 + Try_Dig_b4 + Try_Dig_b5 +
                 Try_Dig_b6 + Try_Dig_b7 + Try_Dig_b8 + Try_Dig_b9 + Try_Dig_b10 +
                 Try_Dig_b11 + Try_Dig_b12 + Try_Dig_b13 + Try_Dig_b14 + Try_Dig_b15 +
                 Try_Dig_b16 + Try_Dig_b17 + Try_Dig_b18 + Try_Dig_b19 + Try_Dig_b20 +
                 plot_layout(ncol = 5, nrow = 4)  # Arrange plots in a grid (5 columns, 4 rows)

# Save the combined plot as a single image
ggsave("../../../R_export/Hv4/trypdig/Combined_Tryptic_Digestion_Efficiency.png", 
       plot = combined_plot, width = 20, height = 16)

```


#Calculate the total number of MS3 spectra acquired per batch

```{r}
#this has not been done but it would be nice to generate a file like that to see the msms IDs 
# List of file paths and batch labels
summary_files <- list(
  "summary_b1" = "../../../R_import/Hv6/summary/summary_b1.txt",
  "summary_b2" = "../../../R_import/Hv6/summary/summary_b2.txt",
  "summary_b3" = "../../../R_import/Hv6/summary/summary_b3.txt",
  "summary_b4" = "../../../R_import/Hv6/summary/summary_b4.txt"
)

# Function to read, clean, and plot a summary file
plot_ms3_per_fraction <- function(filepath, batch_label) {
  data <- read.table(filepath, header = TRUE, sep = "\t")
  data_clean <- data %>%
    filter(!is.na(Fraction)) %>%
    mutate(Fraction = factor(Fraction, levels = 1:48))

  ggplot(data_clean, aes(x = Fraction, y = MS3)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(
      title = paste("MS3 scans (", batch_label, ")", sep = ""),
      x = NULL,  # Removed x-axis label
      y = "MS3 Identifications"
    ) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, size = 6),  # Smaller font
      plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5)
    )
}

# Generate all plots
plots <- mapply(plot_ms3_per_fraction, summary_files, names(summary_files), SIMPLIFY = FALSE)

# Combine using patchwork in a 2x2 layout
combined_plot <- (plots[[1]] | plots[[2]]) / (plots[[3]] | plots[[4]])

# Show combined plot
print(combined_plot)

# Save combined plot
ggsave("../../../R_export/Hv6/ms3_scans/ms3_ID_Hv6.png",
       combined_plot, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300, scale = 0.5)


```

#Calculate the sequence coverage 

```{r}
# Import the pG tables
# Read each file and assign it to a separate object
pG_Hv4_b1_RGT <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_RGT_b1_wFDR.txt", 
                            header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b2_acco <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b2_acco_wFDR.txt", 
                             header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b3_SLA <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b3_SLA_wFDR.txt", 
                            header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b4_OUN <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b4_OUN_wFDR.txt", 
                            header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b5_AA <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b5_AA_wFDR.txt", 
                           header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b6_DUL <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b6_DUL_wFDR.txt", 
                            header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b7_VAL <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b7_VAL_wFDR.txt", 
                            header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b8_MIL <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b8_MIL_wFDR.txt", 
                            header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b9_137 <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b9_137_wFDR.txt", 
                            header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b10_PED <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b10_PED_wFDR.txt", 
                             header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b11_BAR <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b11_BAR_wFDR.txt", 
                             header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b12_PRO <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b12_PRO_wFDR.txt", 
                             header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b13_DIA <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b13_DIA_wFDR.txt", 
                             header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b14_MOR <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b14_MOR_wFDR.txt", 
                             header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b15_TRU <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b15_TRU_wFDR.txt", 
                             header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b16_FT11 <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b16_FT11_wFDR.txt", 
                              header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b17_OPA <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b17_OPA_wFDR.txt", 
                             header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b18_ALE <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b18_ALE_wFDR.txt", 
                             header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b19_KRO <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b19_KRO_wFDR.txt", 
                             header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pG_Hv4_b20_HAN <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/pGroups_Hv4/pGroups_Hv4_b20_HAN_wFDR.txt", 
                             header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Extract column 21 for all batches
seq_cov_data <- data.frame(
  Batch = rep(paste0("Batch ", 1:20), each = nrow(pG_Hv4_b1_RGT)),
  Coverage = c(
    pG_Hv4_b1_RGT[, 21], pG_Hv4_b2_RGT[, 21], pG_Hv4_b3_RGT[, 21], pG_Hv4_b4_RGT[, 21], 
    pG_Hv4_b5_RGT[, 21], pG_Hv4_b6_RGT[, 21], pG_Hv4_b7_RGT[, 21], pG_Hv4_b8_RGT[, 21], 
    pG_Hv4_b9_RGT[, 21], pG_Hv4_b10_RGT[, 21], pG_Hv4_b11_RGT[, 21], pG_Hv4_b12_RGT[, 21],
    pG_Hv4_b13_RGT[, 21], pG_Hv4_b14_RGT[, 21], pG_Hv4_b15_RGT[, 21], pG_Hv4_b16_RGT[, 21],
    pG_Hv4_b17_RGT[, 21], pG_Hv4_b18_RGT[, 21], pG_Hv4_b19_RGT[, 21], pG_Hv4_b20_RGT[, 21]
  )
)

# Generate a violin plot with all 20 batches
ggplot(seq_cov_data, aes(x = Batch, y = Coverage, fill = Batch)) +
  geom_violin(trim = FALSE, color = "black") +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, color = "black", fill = "white", outlier.size = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Sequence Coverage Distribution by Batch",
       x = "Batch",
       y = "Sequence Coverage") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "plain", hjust = 0.5),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "plain"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
        legend.position = "none")
  

```

#Check data distribution of TMT protein groups
This is a useful check to see how data look like before converting to iBAQ

```{r}
#Import all the pG tables
pG_Hv4_RGT_b1 <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/MW_pipe/pG/pGroups_Hv4_RGT_b1_wFDR.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

#Remove potential contaminants, reverse and OIbS.  
pG_Hv4_RGT_b1_noR <- pG_Hv4_RGT_b1[pG_Hv4_RGT_b1$Reverse != "+", ]
pG_Hv4_b2_acco_noR <- pG_Hv4_b2_acco[pG_Hv4_b2_acco$Reverse != "+", ]

##Convert 0 values to 1 
# Function to replace 0 with 1 in specified column range
replace_zeros <- function(df, start_col, end_col) {
  df[, start_col:end_col][df[, start_col:end_col] < 0.1] <- 1
  return(df)
}

# Batch 1
pG_Hv4_RGT_b1_noR <- replace_zeros(pG_Hv4_RGT_b1_noR, 26, 34)

# Batch 2
pG_Hv4_b2_acco_noR <- replace_zeros(pG_Hv4_b2_acco_noR, 35, 43)

# Batch 3
pG_Hv4_b3_SLA_noR <- replace_zeros(pG_Hv4_b3_SLA_noR, 26, 34)

# Batch 4
pG_Hv4_b4_OUN_noR <- replace_zeros(pG_Hv4_b4_OUN_noR, 35, 43)

# Batch 5

# Batch 6

# Batch 14

#Log2(x) transformation
# Batch 1
pG_Hv4_RGT_b1_log2 <- pG_Hv4_RGT_b1_noR %>%
  mutate(
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.1.Hv4_RGT_b1 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.1.Hv4_RGT_b1),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.2.Hv4_RGT_b1 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.2.Hv4_RGT_b1),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.3.Hv4_RGT_b1 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.3.Hv4_RGT_b1),

    Reporter.intensity.corrected.7.Hv4_RGT_b1 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.7.Hv4_RGT_b1),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.8.Hv4_RGT_b1 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.8.Hv4_RGT_b1),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.9.Hv4_RGT_b1 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.9.Hv4_RGT_b1)

# Loop through all log2-transformed data frames
for (var_name in ls(pattern = "^pG_.*_log2$")) {
  df <- get(var_name)
  
  # Convert to data.table if not already
  setDT(df)
  
  # Identify reporter intensity columns
  reporter_cols <- grep("^Reporter\\.intensity\\.corrected\\.", names(df), value = TRUE)
  
  # Perform median centering
  df[, (reporter_cols) := lapply(.SD, function(x) x - median(x, na.rm = TRUE)), .SDcols = reporter_cols]
  
  # Save back to the same variable
  assign(var_name, df, envir = .GlobalEnv)
}
##Save b12 for future comparisons 
fwrite(pG_Hv4_b12_PRO_log2, "../../../R_Data/Hv4/tmt2ibaq/pG_PRO_b12_tmt.csv")

#Histogram that shows data distribution in each sample. The bins are the protein groups. 

# Output directory
output_dir <- "../../../R_export/Hv4/TMT_datasets/data_dist"
dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Define correct columns per batch
column_ranges <- list(
  pG_Hv4_RGT_b1_log2     = 26:34,
  pG_Hv4_b2_acco_log2    = 35:43,
  pG_Hv4_b3_SLA_log2     = 26:34,
  pG_Hv4_b4_OUN_log2     = 35:43,
  pG_Hv4_b5_AA_log2      = 26:34,
  pG_Hv4_b6_DUL_log2     = 35:43,
  pG_Hv4_b7_VAL_log2     = 26:34,
  pG_Hv4_b8_MIL_log2     = 35:43,
  pG_Hv4_b9_137_log2     = 35:43,
  pG_Hv4_b10_PED_log2    = 35:43,
  pG_Hv4_b11_BAR_log2    = 26:34,
  pG_Hv4_b12_PRO_log2    = 35:43,
  pG_Hv4_b13_DIA_log2    = 26:34,
  pG_Hv4_b14_MOR_log2    = 35:43,
  pG_Hv4_b15_TRU_log2    = 26:34,
  pG_Hv4_b16_FT11_log2   = 35:43,
  pG_Hv4_b17_OPA_log2    = 26:34,
  pG_Hv4_b18_ALE_log2    = 35:43,
  pG_Hv4_b19_KRO_log2    = 26:34,
  pG_Hv4_b20_HAN_log2    = 35:43
)

# Loop through all log2-transformed data frames
for (var_name in ls(pattern = "^pG_.*_log2$")) {
  df <- get(var_name)
  setDT(df)

  # Get correct column indices for this batch
  col_range <- column_ranges[[var_name]]
  if (is.null(col_range)) {
    warning(paste("No column range defined for:", var_name))
    next
  }

  # Get corresponding column names
  selected_cols <- names(df)[col_range]

  # Median centering
  df[, (selected_cols) := lapply(.SD, function(x) x - median(x, na.rm = TRUE)), .SDcols = selected_cols]

  # Save back to global environment
  assign(var_name, df, envir = .GlobalEnv)

  # Create histograms only for selected columns
  plots <- lapply(seq_along(selected_cols), function(i) {
    ch <- selected_cols[i]
    ggplot(df, aes_string(x = ch)) +
      geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.7, fill = "#105CB6", color = "black") +
      theme_classic() +
      xlim(-25, 15) +
      labs(x = paste("Channel", i), y = "Count")
  })

  # Create title and arrange plots
  main_title <- textGrob(var_name, gp = gpar(fontsize = 16, fontface = "bold"))
  plot_grid <- arrangeGrob(grobs = plots, ncol = 3, top = main_title)

  # Save plot
  output_path <- file.path(output_dir, paste0(var_name, "_iBAQ_distribution.png"))
  ggsave(output_path, plot = plot_grid, width = 12, height = 9)

  message("Saved distribution plot: ", output_path)
}

##Generate heatmaps 

# Output directory for heatmaps
heatmap_dir <- "../../../R_export/Hv4/TMT_datasets/heatmaps"
dir.create(heatmap_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

)

# Loop through all log2-transformed data frames
for (var_name in ls(pattern = "^pG_.*_log2$")) {
  df <- get(var_name)
  setDT(df)

  # Get correct column indices for this batch
  col_range <- column_ranges[[var_name]]
  if (is.null(col_range)) {
    warning(paste("No column range defined for:", var_name))
    next
  }

  # Get column names for the reporter channels
  available_channels <- names(df)[col_range]

  # Build matrix
  mat_raw <- as.matrix(df[, ..available_channels])
  rownames(mat_raw) <- df$Protein.IDs_pG
  mat_raw <- mat_raw[apply(mat_raw, 1, function(x) sum(!is.na(x)) >= 2), ]

  # Z-score scale by row
  mat_scaled <- t(scale(t(mat_raw)))
  mat_scaled <- mat_scaled[apply(mat_scaled, 1, function(x) all(!is.na(x))), ]

  # Generate and save heatmap
  pheatmap(mat_scaled,
           filename = file.path(heatmap_dir, paste0(var_name, "_heatmap_scaled.png")),
           cluster_rows = TRUE,
           cluster_cols = FALSE,
           show_rownames = FALSE,
           labels_col = paste0("channel", 1:length(available_channels)))

  message("Saved heatmap for: ", var_name)
}




```


#Make a map showing where our varieties come from 
This is useful as an introductory image 

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(rnaturalearth)

# Load the world map
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

# Assign colors to specific countries/regions
world$map_color <- "#E2E0BB"  # Default color for all countries

# Define the attributes for the dots (all varieties)
varieties <- data.frame(
  lon = c(
    2.21, 10.45, 19.15, 84.12, 38.99, 85, 
    18, -3.43, 35.25, -90.71, 10.45, 9, 
    7.8, 11.8, 13.5, 15, 14.9, 16.3, 
    14.0, 35
  ),  # Adjusted longitudes for Germany and Czech Republic
  lat = c(
    46.60, 51.16, 51.91, 28.39, 34.80, 40, 
    49.81, 55.37, 32.95, 37.09, 53.0, 48.5, 
    50.6, 49.0, 50.0, 50.6, 49.2, 49.6, 
    51.0, 32
  ),  # Adjusted latitudes for Germany and Czech Republic
  country_label = c(
    "France", "Germany 1", "Poland", "Nepal", "Syria", "China", 
    "Czech Rep. 1", "UK", "Palestine 1", "US", "Germany 2", "Germany 3", 
    "Germany 4", "Germany 5", "Czech Rep. 2", "Czech Rep. 3", "Czech Rep. 4", "Czech Rep. 5", 
    "Czech Rep. 6", "Palestine 2"
  ),
  color = c(
    "#E41A1C", "#377EB8", "#4DAF4A", "#FF7F00", "#984EA3", "#FFFF33",
    "#A65628", "#F781BF", "#999999", "#66C2A5", "#FC8D62", "#8DA0CB",
    "#E78AC3", "#A6D854", "#FFD92F", "#E5C494", "#B3B3B3", "#1F78B4",
    "#33A02C", "#FB9A99"
  ),  # Colors for the dots
  shape = c(
    21, 18, 18, 17, 17, 17, 
    18, 18, 16, 18, 18, 18, 
    18, 18, 18, 16, 18, 18, 
    18, 18
  ),  # Shape codes for different symbols
  type = c(
    "cultivar", "cultivar", "cultivar", "landrace", "landrace", "landrace",
    "cultivar", "cultivar", "ancestor", "cultivar", "cultivar", "cultivar",
    "cultivar", "cultivar", "cultivar", "cultivar", "cultivar", "cultivar",
    "cultivar", "ancestor"
  ),
  variety = c(
    "RGT Planet", "Accordine", "Slaski", "Chame", "Arabi Aswad", "Du Li Huang",
    "Valticky", "Golden Promise", "B1K0412", "Morex", "Barke","Krona", 
    "Alexis", "Trumpf", "Opavsky Kneifl", "Proskovetz Pedigree", "Diamant", "Proskovcuv Hanna", 
    "Hanà", "HOR13764"
  )  # Variety names for the legend
)

# Create a map with the varieties plotted on it
map <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world, aes(fill = map_color), color = "black") +  # Use the world map
  scale_fill_identity() +
  geom_point(
    data = varieties,
    aes(x = lon, y = lat, color = variety, shape = type),
    size = 2.5,
  show.legend = c(shape = FALSE)  # Suppress the symbol legend
  ) +  # Map variety to both color and shape
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(varieties$color, varieties$variety)) +  # Set specific colors for each variety
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("ancestor" = 17, "landrace" = 18, "cultivar" = 16)) +  # Set triangle (17) for ancestor
  labs(title = "", x = "", y = "") +
  theme_classic() +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-110, 110), ylim = c(5, 75), expand = FALSE) +  # Map limits
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
  panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#A1B9CB"),  # Set ocean/background color to blue
axis.line =  element_blank()) +  # Place legend at the bottom
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(
    title = "Variety", 
    ncol = 6, 
    
    )
  )

# Display the map
print(map)

# Optionally save the plot
ggsave(filename = "varieties_map_maximized_distance.svg", plot = map, device = "svg", path = ".", width = 9, height = 8, scale = 0.9)
ggsave(filename = "varieties_map_maximized_distance.tiff", plot = map, device = "tiff", path = ".", width = 9, height = 8, scale = 0.9)


##Maps for AI 

# --------------------------
# 1) Europa + Israele
# --------------------------
# Load world map
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

map_europe_israel <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world, aes(fill = "#E2E0BB"), color = "black") +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-12, 44), ylim = c(25, 60), expand = FALSE) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#A1B9CB"),
        legend.position = "none")

ggsave("../../../R_export/Hv4/map/map_europe_israel.png", map_europe_israel,
       width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300, scale=0.6)
ggsave("../../../R_export/Hv4/map/map_europe_israel.svg", map_europe_israel,
       width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300, scale=0.6)


# --------------------------
# Full China + neighbors
# --------------------------

# Load world map
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

# Plot full China with surrounding countries
map_china_full <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world, aes(fill = "#E2E0BB"), color = "black") +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  # Coordinates to cover all of China
  coord_sf(xlim = c(70, 140), ylim = c(15, 55), expand = FALSE) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#A1B9CB"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# Save
ggsave("../../../R_export/Hv4/map/map_china_full.png", map_china_full,
       width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300, scale = 0.6)
ggsave("../../../R_export/Hv4/map/map_china_full.svg", map_china_full,
       width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300, scale = 0.6)


# --------------------------
# USA + neighbors (Canada + Mexico)
# --------------------------

# Load world map
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

# Plot USA with surrounding countries
map_usa <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world, aes(fill = "#E2E0BB"), color = "black") +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  # Coordinates to cover USA + Mexico + southern Canada
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-130, -65), ylim = c(20, 55), expand = FALSE) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#A1B9CB"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# Save
ggsave("../../../R_export/Hv4/map/map_usa.png", map_usa,
       width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300, scale = 0.6)
ggsave("../../../R_export/Hv4/map/map_usa.svg", map_usa,
       width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300, scale = 0.6)

```


#Convert TMT values in iBAQ values 
This scripts calculates with a straightforward approach IBAQ values for each protein group. 
What you do is to convert TMT MS2 intensities to MS1 intensities at protein group level and then you calculate IBAQ. 


```{r}
##Load all the files that are required for the pipeline ----
# Batch 1
msms_b1 <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/MW_pipe/msms/msms_b1.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>% rename_with(~paste0(., "_msms"))
peptides_b1 <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/MW_pipe/peptides/peptides_b1.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>% rename_with(~paste0(., "_pep"))
evidence_b1_OF <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/MW_pipe/evidence/evidence2_B1_rgt.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>% rename_with(~paste0(., "_ev"))
pG_b1 <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/MW_pipe/pG/pGroups_Hv4_RGT_b1_wFDR.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>% rename_with(~paste0(., "_pG"))

msms_b19 <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/MW_pipe/msms/msms_b19.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>% rename_with(~paste0(., "_msms"))
peptides_b19 <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/MW_pipe/peptides/peptides_b19.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>% rename_with(~paste0(., "_pep"))
evidence_b19_OF <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/MW_pipe/evidence/evidence2_batch19_KRO.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>% rename_with(~paste0(., "_ev"))
pG_b19 <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/MW_pipe/pG/pGroups_Hv4_b19_KRO_wFDR.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>% rename_with(~paste0(., "_pG"))

# Batch 20
msms_b20 <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/MW_pipe/msms/msms_b20.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>% rename_with(~paste0(., "_msms"))
peptides_b20 <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/MW_pipe/peptides/peptides_b20.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>% rename_with(~paste0(., "_pep"))
evidence_b20_OF <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/MW_pipe/evidence/evidence2_batch20_HAN.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>% rename_with(~paste0(., "_ev"))
pG_b20 <- read.table("../../../R_import/Hv4/MW_pipe/pG/pGroups_Hv4_b20_HAN_wFDR.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>% rename_with(~paste0(., "_pG"))

###Convert MS2 intensities to MS1 intensities ----
#Link each precursor intensity (from evidence.txt) to its corresponding PSM from the msms.txt file
# Batch 1
evi_msms_merged_b1 <- merge(evidence_b1_OF, msms_b1, 
                         by.x = "Best.MS.MS_ev", 
                         by.y = "id_msms", 
                         all.x = TRUE,  
                         all.y = FALSE) 
# Batch 2 ----
evi_msms_merged_b2 <- merge(evidence_b2_OF, msms_b2, 
                         by.x = "Best.MS.MS_ev", 
                         by.y = "id_msms",

                         all.y = FALSE)

##Calculate TMT intensity ratios and redistribute MS1 precursor intensity across channels ----
#Get the intensity cols ----
# Batch 1-20
intensity_cols_b2 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.1[0-8]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b2), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b1 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.[1-9]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b1), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b4 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.1[0-8]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b4), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b3 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.[1-9]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b3), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b6 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.1[0-8]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b6), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b5 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.[1-9]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b5), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b8 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.1[0-8]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b8), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b7 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.[1-9]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b7), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b10 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.1[0-8]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b10), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b9 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.1[0-8]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b9), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b12 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.1[0-8]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b12), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b11 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.[1-9]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b11), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b14 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.1[0-8]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b14), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b13 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.[1-9]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b13), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b16 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.1[0-8]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b16), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b15 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.[1-9]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b15), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b18 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.1[0-8]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b18), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b17 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.[1-9]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b17), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b20 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.1[0-8]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b20), value = TRUE)
intensity_cols_b19 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.[1-9]_ev$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b19), value = TRUE)


#Compute total TMT intensity per precursor (sum of selected channels) ----
#b1
evi_msms_merged_b1 <- evi_msms_merged_b1 %>%
  mutate(Total_Reporter_Intensity = rowSums(select(., all_of(intensity_cols_b1)), na.rm = TRUE))

evi_msms_merged_b1 <- evi_msms_merged_b1 %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(intensity_cols_b1), ~ . / Total_Reporter_Intensity, .names = "{.col}_ratio"))

# Batch 2 ----
evi_msms_merged_b2 <- evi_msms_merged_b2 %>%
  mutate(Total_Reporter_Intensity = rowSums(select(., all_of(intensity_cols_b2)), na.rm = TRUE))

evi_msms_merged_b2 <- evi_msms_merged_b2 %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(intensity_cols_b2), ~ . / Total_Reporter_Intensity, .names = "{.col}_ratio"))

# Compute TMT-corrected intensities by multiplying the ratio with MS1 precursor intensity ----
#b1
ratio_cols_b1 <- grep("_ratio$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b1), value = TRUE)
evi_msms_merged_b1 <- evi_msms_merged_b1 %>%
  mutate(Sum_TMT_Ratios = rowSums(select(., all_of(ratio_cols_b1)), na.rm = TRUE)) # Check that sum is 1
evi_msms_merged_b1 <- evi_msms_merged_b1 %>%
  mutate(across(ends_with("_ratio"), ~ . * Precursor.Intensity_msms, .names = "{.col}_TMT_corrected"))

# Batch 2 ----
ratio_cols_b2 <- grep("_ratio$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b2), value = TRUE)
evi_msms_merged_b2 <- evi_msms_merged_b2 %>%
  mutate(Sum_TMT_Ratios = rowSums(select(., all_of(ratio_cols_b2)), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(across(ends_with("_ratio"), ~ . * Precursor.Intensity_msms, .names = "{.col}_TMT_corrected"))
#quality check to see that conversion provides the expected values  
select(head(evi_msms_merged_b2, 1), Precursor.Intensity_msms)
select(head(evi_msms_merged_b2, 1), Reporter.intensity.corrected.10_ev_ratio)

  mutate(Sum_TMT_Ratios = rowSums(select(., all_of(ratio_cols_b16)), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(across(ends_with("_ratio"), ~ . * Precursor.Intensity_msms, .names = "{.col}_TMT_corrected"))

ratio_cols_b17 <- grep("_ratio$", colnames(evi_msms_merged_b17), value = TRUE)
evi_msms_merged_b17 <- evi_msms_merged_b17 %>%
  mutate(Sum_TMT_Ratios = rowSums(select(., all_of(ratio_cols_b17)), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(across(ends_with("_ratio"), ~ . * Precursor.Intensity_msms, .names = "{.col}_TMT_corrected"))

  mutate(across(ends_with("_ratio"), ~ . * Precursor.Intensity_msms, .names = "{.col}_TMT_corrected"))

#For all peptides sum up all precursor MS1 intensities per channel ----- 
#b1
#Merge peps
peptides_merged_b1 <- merge(peptides_b1, evi_msms_merged_b1, 
                         by.x = "id_pep", by.y = "Peptide.ID_ev",
                         all.x = FALSE,  # Keep only peptides with evidence
                         all.y = TRUE)   # Keep all evidence-linked rows
# Sum MS1 intensities per peptide
tmt_corrected_cols_b1 <- grep("_TMT_corrected$", colnames(peptides_merged_b1), value = TRUE)
# Sum TMT-corrected intensities per peptide
setDT(peptides_merged_b1) #switch it to data table to smooth the summing process 
peptides_summed_b1 <- peptides_merged_b1[, lapply(.SD, sum, na.rm = TRUE), by = id_pep, .SDcols = tmt_corrected_cols_b1]
#Get back all the annotations
peptides_merged_2_b1 <- peptides_merged_b1 %>%
  select(-c(411:419))
peptides_final_b1 <- merge(peptides_summed_b1,peptides_merged_2_b1, by="id_pep", all.x=TRUE, all.y=FALSE)

peptides_final_b1 <- peptides_final_b1[!duplicated(peptides_final_b1$id_pep), ]

peptides_final_b19 <- peptides_final_b19[!duplicated(peptides_final_b19$id_pep), ]
peptides_final_b20 <- peptides_final_b20[!duplicated(peptides_final_b20$id_pep), ]

##Get protein groups values ----
#b1
#Convert to data table format 
setDT(peptides_final_b1)
setDT(pG_b1)

# Identify the intensity columns that need to be summed 
tmt_corrected_cols_b1 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.[0-9]+_ev_ratio_TMT_corrected$", 
                           colnames(peptides_final_b1), value = TRUE)

# Expand 'Evidence.IDs_pG' column so each peptide ID gets its own row
pG_long_b1 <- pG_b1[, .(id_ev = unlist(tstrsplit(Evidence.IDs_pG, ";", fixed = TRUE))), 
                      by = Protein.IDs_pG]
# Convert `id_ev` to numeric, handling potential conversion errors
pG_long_b1[, id_ev := as.integer(id_ev)]

# Merge expanded protein-peptide mapping with peptide intensity values
pG_merged_b1 <- merge(pG_long_b1, 
                   peptides_final_b1[, c("id_ev", tmt_corrected_cols_b1), with = FALSE], 
                   by = "id_ev", 
                   all.x = TRUE)  # Keep all protein-peptide relationships, even if some peptides lack intensities

# Sum peptide intensities for each protein group
pG_summed_b1 <- pG_merged_b1[, lapply(.SD, sum, na.rm = TRUE), 
                        by = Protein.IDs_pG, 
                        .SDcols = tmt_corrected_cols_b1]
# Rename all columns except 'Protein.IDs_pG' by appending "_summed"
setnames(pG_summed_b1, old = tmt_corrected_cols_b1, new = paste0(tmt_corrected_cols_b1, "_summed"))

# Merge summed intensities back into the original protein group file
pG_final_b1 <- merge(pG_b1, pG_summed_b1, by = "Protein.IDs_pG", all.x = TRUE)
pG_final_b1
#Save file
write.csv(pG_final_b1, "../../../R_Data/Hv4/pGroups_MS1/pg_b1_MS1.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Batch 2 ----
setDT(peptides_final_b2)
setDT(pG_b2)

tmt_corrected_cols_b2 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.1[0-8]+_ev_ratio_TMT_corrected$", 
                           colnames(peptides_final_b2), value = TRUE)

pG_long_b2 <- pG_b2[, .(id_ev = unlist(tstrsplit(Evidence.IDs_pG, ";", fixed = TRUE))), 
                      by = Protein.IDs_pG]
pG_long_b2[, id_ev := as.integer(id_ev)]

pG_merged_b2 <- merge(pG_long_b2, 
                   peptides_final_b2[, c("id_ev", tmt_corrected_cols_b2), with = FALSE], 
                   by = "id_ev", 
                   all.x = TRUE)

pG_summed_b2 <- pG_merged_b2[, lapply(.SD, sum, na.rm = TRUE), 
                        by = Protein.IDs_pG, 
                        .SDcols = tmt_corrected_cols_b2]

setnames(pG_summed_b2, old = tmt_corrected_cols_b2, new = paste0(tmt_corrected_cols_b2, "_summed"))

pG_final_b2 <- merge(pG_b2, pG_summed_b2, by = "Protein.IDs_pG", all.x = TRUE)

write.csv(pG_final_b2, "../../../R_Data/Hv4/pGroups_MS1/pg_b2_MS1.csv", row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(pG_final_b3, "../../../R_Data/Hv4/pGroups_MS1/pg_b3_MS1.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Batch 4
setDT(peptides_final_b4)
setDT(pG_b4)

tmt_corrected_cols_b4 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.1[0-8]+_ev_ratio_TMT_corrected$", 
                           colnames(peptides_final_b4), value = TRUE)

pG_long_b4 <- pG_b4[, .(id_ev = unlist(tstrsplit(Evidence.IDs_pG, ";", fixed = TRUE))), 
                      by = Protein.IDs_pG]
pG_long_b4[, id_ev := as.integer(id_ev)]

pG_merged_b4 <- merge(pG_long_b4, 
                   peptides_final_b4[, c("id_ev", tmt_corrected_cols_b4), with = FALSE], 
                   by = "id_ev", 
                   all.x = TRUE)

pG_summed_b4 <- pG_merged_b4[, lapply(.SD, sum, na.rm = TRUE), 
                        by = Protein.IDs_pG, 
                        .SDcols = tmt_corrected_cols_b4]

setnames(pG_summed_b4, old = tmt_corrected_cols_b4, new = paste0(tmt_corrected_cols_b4, "_summed"))

pG_final_b4 <- merge(pG_b4, pG_summed_b4, by = "Protein.IDs_pG", all.x = TRUE)

write.csv(pG_final_b4, "../../../R_Data/Hv4/pGroups_MS1/pg_b4_MS1.csv", row.names = FALSE)

###Convert MS1 intensities to iBAQ intensities ----
##Get tryptic peps per protein ID per fasta file
#RGT
RGT_aafasta <- readAAStringSet("../../../R_import/Hv4/fasta/RGT_Planet.primary.protein.fa")
RGT_peps <- cleave(RGT_aafasta)
RGT_peps_list <- RGT_peps@unlistData

# Barke
Barke_aafasta <- readAAStringSet("../../../R_import/Hv4/fasta/Barke.protein.fa")
Barke_peps <- cleave(Barke_aafasta)
Barke_peps_list <- Barke_peps@unlistData

# FT11_B1K-04-12
FT11_B1K_04_12_aafasta <- readAAStringSet("../../../R_import/Hv4/fasta/FT11_B1K-04-12.protein.fa.fasta")
FT11_B1K_04_12_peps <- cleave(FT11_B1K_04_12_aafasta)
FT11_B1K_04_12_peps_list <- FT11_B1K_04_12_peps@unlistData

# Golden_Promise
Golden_Promise_aafasta <- readAAStringSet("../../../R_import/Hv4/fasta/Golden_Promise.protein.fa.fasta")
Golden_Promise_peps <- cleave(Golden_Promise_aafasta)
Golden_Promise_peps_list <- Golden_Promise_peps@unlistData

# HOR_21599
HOR_21599_aafasta <- readAAStringSet("../../../R_import/Hv4/fasta/HOR_21599.protein.fa.fasta")
HOR_21599_peps <- cleave(HOR_21599_aafasta)
HOR_21599_peps_list <- HOR_21599_peps@unlistData

# HOR_2180
HOR_2180_aafasta <- readAAStringSet("../../../R_import/Hv4/fasta/HOR_2180.protein.fa.fasta")
HOR_2180_peps <- cleave(HOR_2180_aafasta)
HOR_2180_peps_list <- HOR_2180_peps@unlistData

# HOR_3081
HOR_3081_aafasta <- readAAStringSet("../../../R_import/Hv4/fasta/HOR_3081.protein.fa")
HOR_3081_peps <- cleave(HOR_3081_aafasta)
HOR_3081_peps_list <- HOR_3081_peps@unlistData

# HOR_7385

ZDM01467_peps_list <- ZDM01467_peps@unlistData

# Get values ----
# RGT
data_RGT <- data.table(
  Protein_ID = rep(names(RGT_aafasta), lengths(RGT_peps)),  
  Peptide_Seq = as.character(RGT_peps_list),
  Peptide_Length = nchar(as.character(RGT_peps_list))
)[Peptide_Length >= 7 & Peptide_Length <= 40]  

# Barke
data_Barke <- data.table(
  Protein_ID = rep(names(Barke_aafasta), lengths(Barke_peps)),  
  Peptide_Seq = as.character(Barke_peps_list),
  Peptide_Length = nchar(as.character(Barke_peps_list))
)[Peptide_Length >= 7 & Peptide_Length <= 40]

)[Peptide_Length >= 7 & Peptide_Length <= 40]

# Count peptides per protein for each variety ----

# RGT
peptide_counts_RGT <- aggregate(Peptide_Seq ~ Protein_ID, data = data_RGT, FUN = length)
colnames(peptide_counts_RGT) <- c("Protein_ID", "Num_Peptides")
write.csv(peptide_counts_RGT, "../../../R_Data/Hv4/theor_tryp_pep/theor_tryp_pep_RGT_ori.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Barke
peptide_counts_Barke <- aggregate(Peptide_Seq ~ Protein_ID, data = data_Barke, FUN = length)

##Convert MS1 to IBAQ ----
# Convert both data.frames to data.tables for efficient merging
#Load pep counts
peptide_counts_Barke <- read.csv("../../../R_Data/Hv4/theor_tryp_pep/theor_tryp_pep_Barke.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
peptide_counts_RGT <- read.csv("../../../R_Data/Hv4/theor_tryp_pep/theor_tryp_pep_RGT.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

peptide_counts_HOR_21599 <- read.csv("../../../R_Data/Hv4/theor_tryp_pep/theor_tryp_pep_HOR_21599.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

peptide_counts_OUN333 <- read.csv("../../../R_Data/Hv4/theor_tryp_pep/theor_tryp_pep_OUN333.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
peptide_counts_FT11_B1K_04_12 <- read.csv("../../../R_Data/Hv4/theor_tryp_pep/theor_tryp_pep_FT11_B1K_04_12.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Batch 1-20
setDT(pG_final_b1)
setDT(pG_final_b2)
setDT(pG_final_b3)
setDT(pG_final_b4)
setDT(pG_final_b5)
setDT(pG_final_b6)
setDT(pG_final_b7)
setDT(pG_final_b8)
setDT(pG_final_b9)
setDT(pG_final_b10)
setDT(pG_final_b11)
setDT(pG_final_b12)
setDT(pG_final_b13)
setDT(pG_final_b14)
setDT(pG_final_b15)
setDT(pG_final_b16)
setDT(pG_final_b17)
setDT(pG_final_b18)
setDT(pG_final_b19)
setDT(pG_final_b20)

# Convert peptide count data.frames to data.tables for efficient merging
setDT(peptide_counts_RGT)
setDT(peptide_counts_Barke)
setDT(peptide_counts_FT11_B1K_04_12)
setDT(peptide_counts_Golden_Promise)
setDT(peptide_counts_HOR_21599)
setDT(peptide_counts_HOR_2180)
setDT(peptide_counts_HOR_3081)
setDT(peptide_counts_HOR_7385)
setDT(peptide_counts_Morex)
setDT(peptide_counts_OUN333)
setDT(peptide_counts_ZDM01467)


# Batch 1 - RGT
pG_final_b1[, Protein.IDs_pG := sapply(strsplit(Protein.IDs_pG, ";"), `[`, 1)]
pG_final_b1 <- merge(pG_final_b1, peptide_counts_RGT, 
                      by.x = "Protein.IDs_pG", 
                      by.y = "Protein_ID", 
                      all.x = TRUE)

intensity_cols_b1 <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.[0-9]+_ev_ratio_TMT_corrected_summed$", 
                           colnames(pG_final_b1), value = TRUE)


for (col in intensity_cols_b1) {
  new_col_name <- paste0(col, "_iBAQ")
  pG_final_b1[, (new_col_name) := get(col) / Num_Peptides]
}

write.csv(pG_final_b1, "../../../R_Data/Hv4/pGroups_IBAQ/pG_RGT_b1.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

##Quality check on ibaq values 
- with this script I check if the conversion from TMT to ibaq has been succesfull 
- I check data distribution, protein intensity and I perform a correlation between IBAQ and TMT values 

```{r}
# --- Load files ---
data_dir <- "../../../R_Data/Hv4/pGroups_IBAQ/"
file_list <- list.files(path = data_dir, pattern = "^pG_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)

for (file in file_list) {
  var_name <- gsub("^.*/pG_(.*)\\.csv$", "pG_\\1", file)
  assign(var_name, fread(file), envir = .GlobalEnv)
}

# --- Remove Contaminants, Reverse, or OIBS ---
for (var_name in ls(pattern = "^pG_")) {
  df <- get(var_name)
  if (all(c("Reverse", "Potential.contaminant", "Only.identified.by.site") %in% colnames(df))) {
    df <- df[Reverse != "+" & Potential.contaminant != "+" & Only.identified.by.site != "+", ]
  } else if ("Reverse" %in% colnames(df)) {
    df <- df[Reverse != "+", ]
  }
  assign(var_name, df, envir = .GlobalEnv)
}

# --- Log2 Transformation of iBAQ Columns ---
for (var_name in ls(pattern = "^pG_")) {
  df <- get(var_name)
  ibaq_cols <- grep("iBAQ", colnames(df), value = TRUE)

  df[, (ibaq_cols) := lapply(.SD, function(x) {
    x <- as.numeric(as.character(x))
    x[x < 0.1] <- 1  # Set values less than 0.1 to 1
    log2(x)  # Apply log2 directly without adding 1
  }), .SDcols = ibaq_cols]

  assign(var_name, df, envir = .GlobalEnv)
}

# --- Median Centering of iBAQ Columns ---
for (var_name in ls(pattern = "^pG_")) {
  df <- get(var_name)
  ibaq_cols <- grep("iBAQ", colnames(df), value = TRUE)
  df[, (ibaq_cols) := lapply(.SD, function(x) x - median(x, na.rm = TRUE)), .SDcols = ibaq_cols]
  assign(var_name, df, envir = .GlobalEnv)
}

##Just save pG?b12 for future comparisons 
fwrite(pG_PRO_b12, "../../../R_Data/Hv4/tmt2ibaq/pG_PRO_b12_ibaq.csv")


# --- Plotting Distribution and Heatmaps ---
output_dir <- "../../../R_export/Hv4/postibaqconv/iBAQ_Distribution_Plots/"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir)

# Output directory for heatmaps
heatmap_dir <- "../../../R_export/Hv4/postibaqconv/heatmaps"
if (!dir.exists(heatmap_dir)) dir.create(heatmap_dir)

for (var_name in ls(pattern = "^pG_")) {
  df <- get(var_name)

  if (nrow(df) == 0) {
    print(paste("Skipping", var_name, "- dataset is empty."))
    next
  }

  ibaq_cols <- grep("_summed_iBAQ$", colnames(df), value = TRUE)

  if (length(ibaq_cols) == 0) {
    print(paste("Skipping", var_name, "- no iBAQ columns found."))
    next
  }

  # Data Distribution Plot
  plots <- lapply(seq_along(ibaq_cols), function(i) {
    ggplot(df, aes_string(x = ibaq_cols[i])) +
      geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.7, fill = "#105CB6", color = "black") +
      labs(title = paste("Batch", i), x = "Reporter Intensity (iBAQ)", y = "Frequency") +
      theme_classic() +
      xlim(-25, 15) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10)
      )
  })

  if (length(plots) > 0) {
    main_title <- textGrob(var_name, gp = gpar(fontsize = 16, fontface = "bold"))
    plot_grid <- arrangeGrob(grobs = plots, ncol = 3, top = main_title)
    ggsave(filename = paste0(output_dir, var_name, "_iBAQ_distribution.png"),
           plot = plot_grid, width = 12, height = 9)
    print(paste("Saved plot for", var_name))
  }

  # Heatmap Generation
  # Get correct columns for the heatmap (same as for distribution)
  mat_raw <- as.matrix(df[, ..ibaq_cols])
  rownames(mat_raw) <- df$Protein.IDs_pG
  mat_raw <- mat_raw[apply(mat_raw, 1, function(x) sum(!is.na(x)) >= 2), ]

  # Z-score scale by row
  mat_scaled <- t(scale(t(mat_raw)))
  mat_scaled <- mat_scaled[apply(mat_scaled, 1, function(x) all(!is.na(x))), ]

  # Generate and save heatmap
  pheatmap(mat_scaled,
           filename = file.path(heatmap_dir, paste0(var_name, "_heatmap_scaled.png")),
           cluster_rows = TRUE,
           cluster_cols = FALSE,
           show_rownames = FALSE,
           labels_col = paste0("channel", 1:length(ibaq_cols)))

  print(paste("Saved heatmap for", var_name))
}

##Check correlation between plots 
# --- Read iBAQ and TMT data ---
ibaq_df <- fread("../../../R_Data/Hv4/tmt2ibaq/pG_PRO_b12_ibaq.csv")
tmt_df  <- fread("../../../R_Data/Hv4/tmt2ibaq/pG_PRO_b12_tmt.csv")

# --- Clean Protein.IDs in TMT ---
tmt_df[, Protein.IDs := sub(";.*", "", Protein.IDs)]

# --- Define relevant columns ---
tmt_cols  <- paste0("Reporter.intensity.corrected.", 10:18, ".Hv4_b12_pro")
ibaq_cols <- paste0("Reporter.intensity.corrected.", 10:18, "_ev_ratio_TMT_corrected_summed_iBAQ")

# --- Filter out proteins with any value < -18 ---
tmt_df <- tmt_df[rowSums(tmt_df[, ..tmt_cols] < -14, na.rm = TRUE) == 0]
ibaq_df <- ibaq_df[rowSums(ibaq_df[, ..ibaq_cols] < -14, na.rm = TRUE) == 0]

# --- Merge on Protein.IDs ---
merged <- merge(
  tmt_df[, c("Protein.IDs", ..tmt_cols)],
  ibaq_df[, c("Protein.IDs_pG", ..ibaq_cols)],
  by.x = "Protein.IDs", by.y = "Protein.IDs_pG"
)

# --- Rename for clarity ---
colnames(merged) <- c("Protein.ID", paste0("TMT_", 10:18), paste0("iBAQ_", 10:18))

# --- Melt to long format ---
long_df <- melt(merged,
                measure = list(paste0("TMT_", 10:18), paste0("iBAQ_", 10:18)),
                variable.name = "Channel",
                value.name = c("TMT", "iBAQ"))

# --- Remove NA pairs ---
long_df <- na.omit(long_df)

# --- Generate per-channel scatter plots with Pearson r ---
plots <- lapply(unique(long_df$Channel), function(ch) {
  df_ch <- long_df[Channel == ch]
  r <- cor(df_ch$TMT, df_ch$iBAQ, use = "complete.obs")

  ggplot(df_ch, aes(x = TMT, y = iBAQ)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red", se = FALSE, linetype = "dashed") +
    labs(
      title = paste("Channel", ch),
      subtitle = paste("Pearson r =", round(r, 2)),
      x = "TMT (log2, centered)",
      y = "iBAQ (log2, centered)"
    ) +
    theme_classic()
})

# --- Combine and save ---
final_plot <- wrap_plots(plots, ncol = 3)
ggsave("../../../R_export/Hv4/tmt_vs_ibaq_correlation_PRO_b12_filtered.png",
       plot = final_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)


# --- Generate Bland–Altman plots per channel ---
ba_plots <- lapply(unique(long_df$Channel), function(ch) {
  df_ch <- long_df[Channel == ch]
  df_ch$Mean <- rowMeans(df_ch[, .(TMT, iBAQ)], na.rm = TRUE)
  df_ch$Diff <- df_ch$iBAQ - df_ch$TMT
  mean_diff <- mean(df_ch$Diff)
  sd_diff <- sd(df_ch$Diff)

  ggplot(df_ch, aes(x = Mean, y = Diff)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = mean_diff, color = "red", linetype = "solid") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = mean_diff + 1.96 * sd_diff, color = "blue", linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = mean_diff - 1.96 * sd_diff, color = "blue", linetype = "dashed") +
    labs(
      title = paste("Channel", ch),
      subtitle = paste("Mean diff =", round(mean_diff, 2)),
      x = "Mean (TMT + iBAQ) / 2",
      y = "iBAQ – TMT"
    ) +
    theme_classic()
})

# --- Combine and save Bland–Altman plots ---
ba_plot_grid <- wrap_plots(ba_plots, ncol = 3)
ggsave("../../../R_export/Hv4/tmt_vs_ibaq_bland_altman_PRO_b12_filtered.png",
       plot = ba_plot_grid, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)


```

```{r}
selected_rows <- OrthoG_full_iso[1:10, 1:10]

dev.off()
```
