#In this chunk we map intensity values to the orthogroups
#evt it would be nice to see how to include the removed entries 

```{r}
# =============================================================
# Map raw intensity values to orthogroups (Hv4)
# =============================================================

library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# Load all raw CSVs
batch_data_raw <- list()

for (file_path in list.files(input_folder, pattern = "^pG_.*_b[0-9]+\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)) {
  name_clean <- sub("^pG_(.*)_b([0-9]+)\\.csv$", "b\\2_\\1", basename(file_path))
  name_clean <- make.names(name_clean)

  dt <- fread(file_path)

  # Remove reverse hits
  n_before <- nrow(dt)
  dt <- dt[!grepl("REV_", Protein.IDs_pG), ]
  n_after <- nrow(dt)
  cat(sprintf("[%s] Removed %d reverse hits. Remaining: %d\n",
              name_clean, n_before - n_after, n_after))
  
  # Keep protein ID + last 9 iBAQ columns
  if (ncol(dt) < 10) {
    warning(sprintf("⚠️ %s has fewer than 10 columns, skipping.", basename(file_path)))
    next
  }
  keep_cols <- c(1, (ncol(dt) - 8):ncol(dt))
  dt <- dt[, ..keep_cols]

  batch_data_raw[[name_clean]] <- dt
  assign(name_clean, dt, envir = .GlobalEnv)
  cat(sprintf("Loaded raw file: %s with %d intensity cols\n\n", name_clean, length(keep_cols) - 1))
}

# Order list by expected batch names
batch_data_raw <- batch_data_raw[batch_names]
# =============================================================
# STEP 2 — Load Orthogroups and expand with Unassigned genes
# =============================================================
OrthoG_full_iso     <- read_tsv("../../../R_import/Hv4/Ortho/Ortho_Hv4.tsv")
OrthoG_full_iso_MS  <- read_tsv("../../../R_import/Hv4/Ortho/Ortho_Hv4_Spnnagl_0925.tsv")
Unassigned_proteins <- read_tsv("../../../R_import/Hv4/Ortho/Ortho_UA.tsv")
Unassigned_MS       <- read_tsv("../../../R_import/Hv4/Ortho/Ortho_UA_Hv4_Spannagl_0925.tsv")
HO_N1 <- read_tsv("../../../R_import/Hv4/Ortho/N1.tsv")



# Modifz n1
HO_N1 <- dplyr::select(HO_N1, -c(2, 3,15))

# Long-format orthogroup table
variety_cols <- setdiff(colnames(HO_N1), "HOG")

OrthoG_long <- HO_N1 %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(variety_cols), names_to = "Variety", values_to = "Proteins") %>%
  mutate(Proteins = str_remove_all(Proteins, "([^,|]*)\\|")) %>%
  separate_rows(Proteins, sep = ",") %>%
  mutate(Proteins = str_trim(Proteins)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Proteins) & Proteins != "")

# =============================================================
# STEP 3 — Attach pGroup intensities → Orthogroups
# =============================================================
attach_intensities <- function(variety_short, variety_in_ortho, proteome_df, id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG") {
  if (!id_col %in% colnames(proteome_df)) {
    stop(sprintf("For %s: id_col '%s' not found in proteome_df.", variety_short, id_col))
  }
  
  ortho_sub <- OrthoG_long %>% filter(Variety == variety_in_ortho)
  
  numeric_cols <- names(proteome_df)[sapply(proteome_df, is.numeric)]
  ibaq_cols <- setdiff(numeric_cols, id_col)
  if (length(ibaq_cols) == 0) {
    stop(sprintf("For %s: no numeric intensity columns detected.", variety_short))
  }
  
  merged <- proteome_df %>%
    right_join(ortho_sub, by = setNames("Proteins", id_col))
  
  out <- merged %>%
    filter(if_any(all_of(ibaq_cols), ~ !is.na(.))) %>%
    group_by(HOG) %>%
    summarise(
      Proteins = paste(unique(.data[[id_col]]), collapse = ","),
      across(all_of(ibaq_cols), ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  n_ch <- length(ibaq_cols)
  new_names <- c("HOG",
                 paste0(variety_short, "_protein_ID"),
                 paste0("ibaq_", seq_len(n_ch), "_", variety_short))
  names(out) <- new_names
  
  message(sprintf("[%s] OG rows: %d | Channels: %d", variety_short, nrow(out), n_ch))
  return(out)
}

# =============================================================
# STEP 4 — Mapping setup and run
# =============================================================
variety_map <- list(
  RGT  = list(df = b1_RGT,  id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "RGT_Planet.primary"),
  ACCO = list(df = b2_acco, id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "RGT_Planet.primary"),
  SLA  = list(df = b3_SLA,  id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "HOR_3081"),
  OUN  = list(df = b4_OUN,  id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "OUN333"),
  AA   = list(df = b5_AA,   id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "HOR_21599"),
  DUL  = list(df = b6_DUL,  id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "ZDM01467"),
  VAL  = list(df = b7_VAL,  id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "HOR_2180"),
  MIL  = list(df = b8_MIL,  id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "Golden_Promise"),
  `137`= list(df = b9_137,  id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "FT11_B1K-04-12"),
  PED  = list(df = b10_PED, id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "HOR_7385"),
  BAR  = list(df = b11_BAR, id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "Barke"),
  PRO  = list(df = b12_PRO, id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "HOR_2180"),
  DIA  = list(df = b13_DIA, id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "HOR_2180"),
  MOR  = list(df = b14_MOR, id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "Morex"),
  TRU  = list(df = b15_TRU, id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "HOR_2180"),
  FT11 = list(df = b16_FT11, id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "FT11_B1K-04-12"),
  OPA  = list(df = b17_OPA, id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "HOR_2180"),
  ALE  = list(df = b18_ALE, id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "Barke"),
  KRO  = list(df = b19_KRO, id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "Barke"),
  HAN  = list(df = b20_HAN, id_col = "Protein.IDs_pG", variety_in_ortho = "HOR_7385")
)

final_list_raw <- lapply(names(variety_map), function(short) {
  attach_intensities(
    variety_short    = short,
    variety_in_ortho = variety_map[[short]]$variety_in_ortho,
    proteome_df      = variety_map[[short]]$df,
    id_col           = variety_map[[short]]$id_col
  )
})
names(final_list_raw) <- names(variety_map)

# =============================================================
# STEP 5 — Merge, filter, reorder columns
# =============================================================
final_merged_raw <- Reduce(function(x, y) full_join(x, y, by = "HOG"), final_list_raw)

ibaq_cols_all <- grep("^ibaq_[0-9]+_", colnames(final_merged_raw), value = TRUE)
keep_rows <- rowSums(!is.na(final_merged_raw[, ibaq_cols_all])) > 0
final_merged_filtered <- final_merged_raw[keep_rows, ]

desired_order <- c("FT11", "137", "AA", "OUN", "DUL", "HAN", "PED", "PRO", "VAL",
                   "OPA", "DIA", "TRU", "ALE", "KRO", "MOR", "SLA", "MIL", "BAR",
                   "ACCO", "RGT")

ordered_columns <- c("HOG")
for (v in desired_order) {
  id_col_v    <- grep(paste0("^", v, "_protein_ID$"), colnames(final_merged_filtered), value = TRUE)
  ibaq_cols_v <- grep(paste0("^ibaq_[0-9]+_", v, "$"), colnames(final_merged_filtered), value = TRUE)
  ordered_columns <- c(ordered_columns, id_col_v, ibaq_cols_v)
}
ordered_columns <- unique(ordered_columns)

final_ordered <- final_merged_filtered[, ordered_columns]
heatmap_data  <- final_ordered[, grep("^ibaq_[0-9]+_", colnames(final_ordered))]

# =============================================================
# STEP 6 — Save
# =============================================================
out_dir <- "../../../R_Data/Hv4/postnorm2"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

write.csv(final_ordered, file.path(out_dir, "full_ortho_intensities_intra_with_ID.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(heatmap_data, file.path(out_dir, "full_ortho_intensities_intra.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

message(sprintf("Final OG rows: %d ; total intensity columns: %d",
                nrow(final_ordered), ncol(heatmap_data)))

# =============================================================
# STEP 7 — Consistency check
# =============================================================
check_losses <- function(variety) {
  proteome_df <- variety_map[[variety]]$df
  id_col      <- variety_map[[variety]]$id_col
  
  orig_n <- proteome_df %>%
    distinct(.data[[id_col]]) %>%
    nrow()
  
  final_ids <- final_list_raw[[variety]] %>%
    separate_rows(!!sym(paste0(variety, "_protein_ID")), sep = ",") %>%
    mutate(tmp_id = str_trim(!!sym(paste0(variety, "_protein_ID")))) %>%
    distinct(tmp_id) %>%
    nrow()
  
  tibble(
    Variety   = variety,
    Original  = orig_n,
    Included  = final_ids,
    Lost      = orig_n - final_ids
  )
}

loss_summary <- bind_rows(lapply(names(variety_map), check_losses))
print(loss_summary)

# =============================================================
# RGT — Heatmaps Before and After Orthogroup Mapping
# =============================================================
library(data.table)
library(pheatmap)

# =============================================================
# STEP 1 — Create output folders
# =============================================================
out_dir_before <- "../../../R_export/Hv4/orthology/RGT_before"
out_dir_after  <- "../../../R_export/Hv4/orthology/RGT_after"
dir.create(out_dir_before, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(out_dir_after,  recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# =============================================================
# STEP 2 — RGT BEFORE merging (raw file)
# =============================================================
cat("\n--- Generating RGT heatmap BEFORE orthogroup mapping ---\n")

# Take b1_RGT from global environment (already loaded earlier)
rgt_raw <- copy(b1_RGT)

# Select only numeric iBAQ columns (last 9 columns)
ibaq_cols_rgt <- tail(names(rgt_raw), 9)
mat_before <- as.matrix(rgt_raw[, ..ibaq_cols_rgt])

# Convert to numeric and preprocess
mode(mat_before) <- "numeric"
mat_before[!is.finite(mat_before)] <- NA
mat_before_log <- log2(mat_before + 1)

# Replace NA or <0.1 with 3
mat_before_log[is.na(mat_before_log) | mat_before_log < log2(0.1 + 1)] <- 3

# Remove rows with zero variance
vars_before <- apply(mat_before_log, 1, var, na.rm = TRUE)
n_zero_var_before <- sum(is.na(vars_before) | vars_before == 0)
mat_before_log <- mat_before_log[vars_before > 0 & !is.na(vars_before), ]

cat(sprintf("⚠️ Removed %d rows with zero variance (before mapping)\n", n_zero_var_before))

# Generate and save heatmap
if (nrow(mat_before_log) > 1) {
  pheatmap(
    mat_before_log,
    filename = file.path(out_dir_before, "heatmap_RGT_before.png"),
    main = "RGT — Before Orthogroup Mapping",
    scale = "row",
    show_rownames = FALSE,
    border_color = NA,
    cluster_cols = FALSE,
    clustering_method = "average",
    silent = TRUE,
    width = 10,
    height = 8
  )
  cat("✅ Saved heatmap: RGT_before\n")
} else {
  cat("⚠️ Skipped RGT_before heatmap: not enough variable rows.\n")
}

# =============================================================
# STEP 3 — RGT AFTER merging (from final_ordered)
# =============================================================
cat("\n--- Generating RGT heatmap AFTER orthogroup mapping ---\n")

# Extract RGT iBAQ columns from final_ordered
ibaq_cols_rgt_after <- grep("^ibaq_[0-9]+_RGT$", colnames(final_ordered), value = TRUE)
rgt_after <- copy(final_ordered)[, ibaq_cols_rgt_after, with = FALSE]

# Remove rows where all 9 channels are NA or <0.1
keep_rows <- apply(rgt_after, 1, function(x) any(!is.na(x) & x >= 0.1))
rgt_after <- rgt_after[keep_rows, ]

# Convert to numeric matrix
mat_after <- as.matrix(rgt_after)
mode(mat_after) <- "numeric"
mat_after[!is.finite(mat_after)] <- NA

# Log2(+1) transform
mat_after_log <- log2(mat_after + 1)

# Replace NA or <0.1 (in log2 space) with 3
mat_after_log[is.na(mat_after_log) | mat_after_log < log2(0.1 + 1)] <- 3

# Remove rows with zero variance
vars_after <- apply(mat_after_log, 1, var, na.rm = TRUE)
n_zero_var_after <- sum(is.na(vars_after) | vars_after == 0)
mat_after_log <- mat_after_log[vars_after > 0 & !is.na(vars_after), ]

cat(sprintf("⚠️ Removed %d rows with zero variance (after mapping)\n", n_zero_var_after))

# Generate and save heatmap
if (nrow(mat_after_log) > 1) {
  pheatmap(
    mat_after_log,
    filename = file.path(out_dir_after, "heatmap_RGT_after.png"),
    main = "RGT — After Orthogroup Mapping",
    scale = "row",
    show_rownames = FALSE,
    border_color = NA,
    cluster_cols = FALSE,
    clustering_method = "average",
    silent = TRUE,
    width = 10,
    height = 8
  )
  cat("✅ Saved heatmap: RGT_after\n")
} else {
  cat("⚠️ Skipped RGT_after heatmap: not enough variable rows.\n")
}

# =============================================================
# STEP 4 — Summary
# =============================================================
cat("\n✅ Heatmap generation complete.\n")
cat(sprintf("Before mapping: %d proteins (after variance filter)\n", nrow(mat_before_log)))
cat(sprintf("After mapping:  %d orthogroups (after variance filter)\n", nrow(mat_after_log)))
cat(sprintf("Outputs saved in:\n  %s\n  %s\n", out_dir_before, out_dir_after))


```

##Intra and inter batch normalisation 
We checked and proteins are correcltz attached to orthogroups, without altering data structure 
here we load raw data merged to orthogroups and inter and intra batch normalise them 

```{r}
# =============================================================
# FULL PIPELINE with Edge-Replicated Moving Average (k=3)
# =============================================================

# -----------------------------
# Libraries
# -----------------------------
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(pheatmap)
library(magick)

# -----------------------------
# PARAMETERS
# -----------------------------
file_path    <- "../../../R_Data/Hv4/postnorm2/full_ortho_intensities_intra_with_ID.csv"
out_dir_base <- "../../../R_export/Hv4/single"
out_data_dir <- "../../../R_Data/Hv4/single"
missing_limit <- 7
min_val <- 0.1

dir.create(out_dir_base, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(out_data_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# =============================================================
# BATCH MAPPING (name → threshold)
# =============================================================
batch_thresholds <- list(
    "RGT"  = 2, "ACCO" = 2, "AA"   = 2, "DUL"  = 1.8, "VAL"  = 2,
    "MIL"  = 2, "PED"  = 1.8, "PRO"  = 2, "DIA"  = 3.5, "MOR"  = 1.6,
    "ALE"  = 2, "KRO"  = 1.8, "HAN"  = 1.8, "SLA"  = 3, "OUN"  = 2,
    "BAR"  = 1.8, "TRU"  = 2, "FT11" = 2.5, "OPA"  = 1.8, "137"  = 1.6
)

# =============================================================
# OPTIONAL: batch-specific channel correction rules
# =============================================================
batch_specific_rules <- list(
    "ACCO" = list(list(target = 5, neighbors = c(4,6))),
    "TRU"  = list(
        list(target = 3, neighbors = c(2,4)),
        list(target = 5, neighbors = c(4,6)),
        list(target = 8, neighbors = c(7,9))
    ),
    "RGT"  = list(list(target = 1, neighbors = c(2))),
    "PRO"  = list(list(target = 3, neighbors = c(2,4))),
    "DUL"  = list(list(target = 8, neighbors = c(7,9))),
    "MOR"  = list(list(target = 8, neighbors = c(7,9))),
    "PED"  = list(
        list(target = 4, neighbors = c(3)),
        list(target = 6, neighbors = c(7)),
        list(target = 5, neighbors = c(7))
    ),
    "OPA"  = list(
        list(target = 3, neighbors = c(2)),
        list(target = 5, neighbors = c(6))
    ),
    "137"  = list(
        list(target = 6, neighbors = c(7)),
        list(target = 4, neighbors = c(3)),
        list(target = 5, neighbors = c(7))
    ),
    "HAN"  = list(
        list(target = 3, neighbors = c(2)),
        list(target = 4, neighbors = c(2)),
        list(target = 5, neighbors = c(6))
    ),
    "DIA"  = list(list(target = 2, neighbors = c(1,3))),
    "KRO"  = list(
      list(target = 1, neighbors = c(2)),
      list(target = 2, neighbors = c(3)),
      list(target = 4, neighbors = c(3,5)),
        list(target = 5, neighbors = c(4,6))
    )
)

# =============================================================
# Helper Functions — QC plot & Heatmap
# =============================================================
make_qc_plot <- function(df, title, intensity_cols) {
    sums    <- colSums(df[, ..intensity_cols], na.rm = TRUE)
    medians <- vapply(intensity_cols, function(cn) median(df[[cn]], na.rm = TRUE), numeric(1))
    stats_df <- data.table(Channel=factor(paste0("Ch", seq_along(intensity_cols))),
                           Total_Intensity=as.numeric(sums),
                           Median_Intensity=as.numeric(medians))
    max_total <- max(stats_df$Total_Intensity, na.rm = TRUE)
    max_med   <- max(stats_df$Median_Intensity,  na.rm = TRUE)
    scale_factor <- if (is.finite(max_total / max_med) && max_med > 0) max_total / max_med else 1
    ggplot(stats_df, aes(x = Channel)) +
        geom_bar(aes(y = Total_Intensity), stat = "identity", fill="steelblue", alpha=0.6) +
        geom_point(aes(y = Median_Intensity * scale_factor), color="red", size=2.5) +
        geom_line(aes(y = Median_Intensity * scale_factor, group=1), color="red", linewidth=0.7) +
        scale_y_continuous(name="Total Intensity",
                           sec.axis = sec_axis(~./scale_factor, name="Median Intensity")) +
        labs(title=title) + theme_classic()
}

make_heatmap <- function(df, title, intensity_cols, outfile = NA) {
    heat_colors <- colorRampPalette(c("#4579F1", "white", "#D73020"))(130)
    mat <- as.matrix(df[, ..intensity_cols])
    mode(mat) <- "numeric"
    mat[!is.finite(mat)] <- NA
    mat_log <- log10(mat + 1)
    vars <- apply(mat_log, 1, var, na.rm = TRUE)
    keep <- is.finite(vars) & vars > 0
    mat_log <- mat_log[keep, , drop = FALSE]
    if (nrow(mat_log) < 2) {
        message("⚠️ Skipped heatmap: not enough variable rows for ", title)
        return(invisible(NULL))
    }
    colnames(mat_log) <- paste0("Ch", seq_along(intensity_cols))
    pheatmap(
        mat_log,
        filename = outfile,
        main = title,
        scale = "row",
        show_rownames = FALSE,
        border_color = NA,
        cluster_cols = FALSE,
        fontsize=16,
        clustering_method = "average",
        silent = TRUE,
        color = heat_colors,
        width = 12, height = 10
    )
}

# =============================================================
# NEW: Edge-Replicated Moving Average Smoothing (k=3)
# =============================================================
smooth_transition <- function(x, k = 3) {
    if (all(is.na(x))) return(x)
    # replicate edges to preserve boundary shape
    x_ext <- c(rep(x[1], floor(k/2)), x, rep(x[length(x)], floor(k/2)))
    y <- stats::filter(x_ext, rep(1 / k, k), sides = 2)
    y <- y[(floor(k/2)+1):(length(y)-floor(k/2))]  # trim back to original length
    as.numeric(y)
}

smooth_matrix <- function(mat, k = 3) {
    t(apply(mat, 1, smooth_transition, k = k))
}

# =============================================================
# CORE FUNCTION — run_batch_norm()
# =============================================================
run_batch_norm <- function(batch_name, threshold) {
    cat("\n============================\n")
    cat(sprintf(">>> Processing batch: %s (threshold = %s)\n", batch_name, threshold))
    cat("============================\n")
    
    dt <- fread(file_path)
    cols   <- grep(paste0("^ibaq_\\d+_", batch_name, "$"), colnames(dt), value = TRUE)
    id_col <- grep(paste0(batch_name, "_protein_ID"), colnames(dt), value = TRUE)
    if (length(cols) == 0 || length(id_col) == 0) {
        cat(sprintf("⚠️ No columns found for batch '%s'. Skipping.\n", batch_name))
        return(invisible(NULL))
    }
    
    sub_dt <- dt[, c("HOG", id_col, cols), with = FALSE]
    keep_rows <- apply(sub_dt[, ..cols], 1, function(x) sum(is.na(x) | x < min_val) < missing_limit)
    sub_dt <- sub_dt[keep_rows]
    
    export_dir <- file.path(out_dir_base, paste0(batch_name, "_from_ortho_single"))
    dir.create(export_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
    
    # --- Pre-normalization plots
    p_bar_pre <- make_qc_plot(sub_dt, paste("Total & Median Intensity — pre-normalization (", batch_name, ")", sep = ""), cols)
    ggsave(file.path(export_dir, paste0("barplot_preNorm_", batch_name, ".png")), p_bar_pre, width = 10, height = 5, dpi = 150)
    make_heatmap(sub_dt, paste("Heatmap — pre-normalization (", batch_name, ")", sep = ""),
                 cols, outfile = file.path(export_dir, paste0("heatmap_preNorm_", batch_name, ".png")))
    
    # --- Adaptive imputation & shrinkage ---
    n_imputed <- 0
    n_corrected <- 0
    imputed_mask <- matrix(FALSE, nrow = nrow(sub_dt), ncol = length(cols))
    colnames(imputed_mask) <- cols
    
    for (i in seq_len(nrow(sub_dt))) {
        row <- unlist(sub_dt[i, ..cols])
        rules <- batch_specific_rules[[batch_name]]
        
        # Apply batch-specific rules
        if (!is.null(rules)) {
            for (rule in rules) {
                target <- rule$target
                neigh_idx <- rule$neighbors
                if (all(neigh_idx %in% seq_along(row))) {
                    val <- row[target]
                    neigh <- row[neigh_idx]
                    neigh <- neigh[is.finite(neigh) & neigh > 0]
                    if (length(neigh)) {
                        val_neigh <- mean(neigh, na.rm = TRUE)
                        if (is.na(val) || val < min_val) {
                            row[target] <- val_neigh
                            imputed_mask[i, target] <- TRUE
                            n_imputed <- n_imputed + 1
                        } else {
                            log2fc <- log2((val + 1) / (val_neigh + 1))
                            if (abs(log2fc) > threshold) {
                                row[target] <- val_neigh
                                n_corrected <- n_corrected + 1
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        
        # Default correction for remaining channels
        for (j in seq_along(row)) {
            if (!is.null(rules) && j %in% vapply(rules, function(x) x$target, numeric(1))) next
            val <- row[j]
            neigh <- row[c(j - 1, j + 1)]
            neigh <- neigh[is.finite(neigh) & neigh > 0]
            if (!length(neigh)) next
            val_neigh <- mean(neigh, na.rm = TRUE)
            if (is.na(val) || val < min_val) {
                row[j] <- val_neigh
                imputed_mask[i, j] <- TRUE
                n_imputed <- n_imputed + 1
            } else {
                log2fc <- log2((val + 1) / (val_neigh + 1))
                if (abs(log2fc) > threshold) {
                    row[j] <- val_neigh
                    n_corrected <- n_corrected + 1
                }
            }
        }
        
        sub_dt[i, (cols) := as.list(row)]
    }
    
    cat(sprintf("✅ %s — Total corrected: %d (imputed: %d, replaced: %d)\n",
                batch_name, n_imputed + n_corrected, n_imputed, n_corrected))
    
    # --- Intra-batch total-sum normalization (TSN)
    col_sums <- colSums(sub_dt[, ..cols], na.rm = TRUE)
    grand_median <- median(col_sums, na.rm = TRUE)
    factors <- grand_median / col_sums
    
    for (j in seq_along(cols)) {
        colname <- cols[j]
        x <- sub_dt[[colname]]
        mask <- imputed_mask[, j]
        x[!mask & is.finite(x)] <- x[!mask & is.finite(x)] * factors[j]
        sub_dt[[colname]] <- x
    }
    
    # --- NEW STEP: Edge-Replicated Moving Average Smoothing ---
    mat_postTSN <- as.matrix(sub_dt[, ..cols])
    mat_smooth  <- smooth_matrix(mat_postTSN, k = 3)
    sub_dt[, (cols) := as.data.table(mat_smooth)]
    cat(sprintf("✨ %s — Applied edge-replicated moving-average smoothing (k=3)\n", batch_name))
    
    # --- Post-normalization plots
    p_bar_post <- make_qc_plot(sub_dt, paste("Total & Median Intensity — post-normalization (", batch_name, ")", sep = ""), cols)
    ggsave(file.path(export_dir, paste0("barplot_postNorm_", batch_name, ".png")), p_bar_post, width = 10, height = 5, dpi = 150)
    make_heatmap(sub_dt, paste("Heatmap — post-normalization (", batch_name, ")", sep = ""),
                 cols, outfile = file.path(export_dir, paste0("heatmap_postNorm_", batch_name, ".png")))
    
    # --- Save normalized (log2+1) data
    for (c in cols) sub_dt[[c]] <- log2(sub_dt[[c]] + 1)
    out_file <- file.path(out_data_dir, paste0("full_ortho_", batch_name, "_norm.csv"))
    fwrite(sub_dt, out_file)
    return(sub_dt)
}

# =============================================================
# RUN ALL BATCHES
# =============================================================
merged_list <- list()
for (b in names(batch_thresholds)) {
    res <- try(run_batch_norm(b, batch_thresholds[[b]]), silent = TRUE)
    if (!inherits(res, "try-error") && !is.null(res)) merged_list[[b]] <- res
}

# =============================================================
# MERGE ALL BATCHES
# =============================================================
merged_dt <- Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = "HOG", all = TRUE), merged_list)
merged_outfile <- file.path(out_data_dir, "Hv4_allBatches_merged_byHOG.csv")
fwrite(merged_dt, merged_outfile)
cat(sprintf("\n✅ Combined dataset saved: %s\n", merged_outfile))
cat(sprintf("Merged dimensions: %d rows × %d columns\n", nrow(merged_dt), ncol(merged_dt)))

# =============================================================
# STEP 2 — INTER-BATCH MEDIAN CENTERING
# =============================================================
cat("\n--- Performing inter-batch median centering ---\n")
out_inter_dir  <- "../../../R_Data/Hv4/single_inter"
out_plot_inter <- "../../../R_export/Hv4/single_inter"
dir.create(out_inter_dir,  recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(out_plot_inter, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

batch_names <- names(batch_thresholds)
batch_medians <- sapply(batch_names, function(v) {
    cols_v <- grep(paste0("_", v, "$"), names(merged_dt), value = TRUE)
    vals <- unlist(merged_dt[, ..cols_v])
    vals <- vals[is.finite(vals)]
    median(vals, na.rm = TRUE)
})

grand_median <- median(batch_medians, na.rm = TRUE)
cor_factors  <- grand_median / batch_medians

merged_corrected <- copy(merged_dt)
for (v in names(cor_factors)) {
    f <- cor_factors[v]
    cols_v <- grep(paste0("_", v, "$"), names(merged_corrected), value = TRUE)
    merged_corrected[, (cols_v) := lapply(.SD, function(x) x * f), .SDcols = cols_v]
}

out_file_corrected <- file.path(out_inter_dir, "Hv4_allBatches_interBatchNorm.csv")
fwrite(merged_corrected, out_file_corrected)
cat(sprintf("✅ Saved inter-batch data → %s\n", out_file_corrected))

# =============================================================
# QC PLOTS FOR INTER-BATCH
# =============================================================
for (v in batch_names) {
    cols_v <- grep(paste0("_", v, "$"), names(merged_corrected), value = TRUE)
    if (length(cols_v) == 0) next
    df_v <- merged_corrected[, ..cols_v]
    export_dir <- file.path(out_plot_inter, paste0(v, "_postInter"))
    dir.create(export_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
    
    p_bar <- make_qc_plot(df_v, paste("Total & Median Intensity — post-inter (", v, ")", sep = ""), cols_v)
    ggsave(file.path(export_dir, paste0("barplot_postInter_", v, ".png")), p_bar, width = 10, height = 5, dpi = 150)
    make_heatmap(df_v, paste("Heatmap — post-inter (", v, ")", sep = ""),
                 cols_v, outfile = file.path(export_dir, paste0("heatmap_postInter_", v, ".png")))
    cat(sprintf("Saved post-inter plots for %s\n", v))
}

cat("\n✅ All batches normalized successfully (with batch-specific corrections applied).\n")

# =============================================================
# STEP 5 — Row-wise median normalization for BARKE (BAR)
# =============================================================
cat("\n--- Applying row-wise median normalization for BARKE (BAR) ---\n")

final_corrected <- copy(merged_corrected)
ordered_ibaq_cols <- grep("^ibaq_", names(final_corrected), value = TRUE)
desired_order <- unique(sub(".*_(\\w+)$", "\\1", ordered_ibaq_cols))
cols_barke <- grep("_BAR$", ordered_ibaq_cols, value = TRUE)

get_barke_factor <- function(row_values, cols, varieties) {
    means_per_variety <- c()
    for (v in varieties) {
        cols_v <- grep(paste0("_", v, "$"), cols, value = TRUE)
        values_v <- as.numeric(row_values[cols_v])
        if (all(values_v >= 0.1, na.rm = TRUE)) {
            means_per_variety[v] <- mean(values_v, na.rm = TRUE)
        } else {
            means_per_variety[v] <- NA
        }
    }
    row_median <- median(means_per_variety, na.rm = TRUE)
    barke_mean <- means_per_variety["BAR"]
    if (!is.na(barke_mean)) return(row_median / barke_mean) else return(NA)
}

barke_factors <- apply(final_corrected[, ..ordered_ibaq_cols], 1,
                       get_barke_factor,
                       cols = ordered_ibaq_cols,
                       varieties = desired_order)

corrected_mat <- sweep(as.matrix(final_corrected[, ..cols_barke]),
                       1, barke_factors,
                       FUN = function(x, f) ifelse(is.na(f), x, x * f))
final_corrected[, (cols_barke) := as.data.table(corrected_mat)]

cat(sprintf("✔ Row-wise median normalization applied to %d BARKE columns.\n", length(cols_barke)))

out_file_corrected <- file.path(out_inter_dir, "Hv4_allBatches_interBatchNorm.csv")
fwrite(final_corrected, out_file_corrected)
cat("✅ Final file overwritten with BARKE row-wise median normalization:\n   ", out_file_corrected, "\n")

# =============================================================
# COLLAGE CREATION — Heatmaps & Barplots
# =============================================================
cat("\n--- Creating collage of all post-inter normalization heatmaps ---\n")

heatmap_files <- c()
for (v in batch_names) {
    hm_path <- file.path(out_plot_inter, paste0(v, "_postInter"), paste0("heatmap_postInter_", v, ".png"))
    if (file.exists(hm_path)) heatmap_files <- c(heatmap_files, hm_path)
}

if (length(heatmap_files) > 0) {
    heatmaps <- lapply(heatmap_files, magick::image_read)
    collage <- magick::image_montage(
        magick::image_join(heatmaps),
        tile = "5x",
        geometry = "+5+5"
    )
    collage <- magick::image_border(collage, color = "white", geometry = "5x5")
    collage_file <- file.path(out_plot_inter, "AllBatches_postInter_HeatmapCollage.png")
    magick::image_write(collage, collage_file, format = "png")
    cat(sprintf("✅ Collage saved: %s\n", collage_file))
}

cat("\n--- Creating collage of all post-inter normalization barplots ---\n")

barplot_files <- c()
for (v in batch_names) {
    hm_path <- file.path(out_plot_inter, paste0(v, "_postInter"), paste0("barplot_postInter_", v, ".png"))
    if (file.exists(hm_path)) barplot_files <- c(barplot_files, hm_path)
}

if (length(barplot_files) > 0) {
    barplots <- lapply(barplot_files, magick::image_read)
    collage <- magick::image_montage(
        magick::image_join(barplots),
        tile = "5x",
        geometry = "+5+5"
    )
    collage <- magick::image_border(collage, color = "white", geometry = "5x5")
    collage_file <- file.path(out_plot_inter, "AllBatches_postInter_BarplotCollage.png")
    magick::image_write(collage, collage_file, format = "png")
    cat(sprintf("✅ Collage saved: %s\n", collage_file))
}


#Create a bar plot with stats
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# =============================================================
# Define data
# =============================================================
batch_stats <- data.frame(
  Batch = c("RGT","ACCO","AA","DUL","VAL","MIL","PED","PRO","DIA","MOR",
            "ALE","KRO","HAN","SLA","OUN","BAR","TRU","FT11","OPA","137"),
  Proteins = c(8690, 8799, 8523, 7969, 8185, 8583, 6772, 8252, 7493, 8675,
               7311, 7059, 7400, 8954, 8334, 8001, 6908, 7399, 7074, 7149),
  Imputed = c(907,288,543,382,412,1104,1671,665,254,464,
              435,799,2283,385,477,457,2472,1667,526,1868),
  Outliers = c(3439,3560,394,1338,392,1942,7334,2233,423,647,
               691,2263,11087,302,956,2145,8366,7216,1050,11541)
)

# Multiply proteins by 9 (9 time points)
batch_stats <- batch_stats %>%
  mutate(Total_Values = Proteins * 9)

# Calculate the "remaining" uncorrected values
batch_stats <- batch_stats %>%
  mutate(Uncorrected = Total_Values - Imputed - Outliers)

# =============================================================
# Prepare long-format data
# =============================================================
plot_df <- batch_stats %>%
  select(Batch, Uncorrected, Imputed, Outliers) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Batch, names_to = "Category", values_to = "Count") %>%
  mutate(Category = factor(Category, levels = c("Uncorrected", "Imputed", "Outliers")),
         Batch = factor(Batch, levels = batch_stats$Batch))

# =============================================================
# Plot stacked bars
# =============================================================
ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Batch, y = Count, fill = Category)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Uncorrected" = "#4E79A7",  # blue
                               "Imputed" = "orange2",      # orange
                               "Outliers" = "grey")) +  # red
  labs(
    x = "Batch", y = "# of intensity values"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.title = element_blank()
  )

```

#Attach functional annotations 

```{r}
# =============================================================
# 1. Load orthogroups with intensities (post-inter batch normalization)
# =============================================================
full_ortho <- read.csv(
  "../../../R_Data/Hv4/single_inter/Hv4_allBatches_interBatchNorm.csv",
  header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Fix naming inconsistencies
colnames(full_ortho) <- gsub("^X137_protein_ID$", "137_protein_ID", colnames(full_ortho))
colnames(full_ortho) <- gsub("^HOG$", "Orthogroup", colnames(full_ortho))

# =============================================================
# 2. Generic function to process Diamond matches
# =============================================================
library(tidyverse)

process_matches <- function(filepath, prefix, species) {
  col1 <- paste0(prefix, "2", species)
  col2 <- paste0(species, "2", prefix)
  
  df <- read_tsv(filepath, col_names = FALSE, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    select(X1, X2) %>%
    distinct(X1, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
    mutate(X2 = str_extract(X2, "[^|]+$"))
  
  colnames(df) <- c(col1, col2)
  return(df)
}

# =============================================================
# 3. Load match files
# =============================================================
species <- c("Hv", "Ta", "At")

prefixes <- c(
  "BK", "FT11", "HOR21599", "HOR2180", "HOR3081", "HOR7385",
  "MIL", "Morex", "OUN333", "RGT", "ZDM01467"
)

for (sp in species) {
  for (pre in prefixes) {
    assign(
      paste0(pre, "_vs_", sp),
      process_matches(
        paste0("../../../R_import/Hv4/func_anno/", sp, "_match/", pre, "_", sp, "_match.tsv"),
        pre, sp
      )
    )
  }
}

# =============================================================
# 4. Load UniProt annotation masters
# =============================================================
At_UP_annotated <- read_tsv("../../../R_import/Hv4/func_anno/masters/unip_At_1505.tsv")
Ta_UP_annotated <- read_tsv("../../../R_import/Hv4/func_anno/masters/unip_Ta_1505.tsv")
Hv_UP_annotated <- read_tsv("../../../R_import/Hv4/func_anno/masters/unip_Hv_1505.tsv")

# =============================================================
# 5. Annotate matches
# =============================================================
annotate_vs <- function(match_df, annot_df) {
  match_col <- colnames(match_df)[2]
  merge(match_df, annot_df, by.x = match_col, by.y = "Entry Name", all.x = TRUE)
}

vs_Hv_names <- paste0(prefixes, "_vs_Hv")
vs_Ta_names <- paste0(prefixes, "_vs_Ta")
vs_At_names <- paste0(prefixes, "_vs_At")

for (obj in vs_Hv_names) {
  assign(paste0(obj, "_annotated"), annotate_vs(get(obj), Hv_UP_annotated))
}
for (obj in vs_Ta_names) {
  assign(paste0(obj, "_annotated"), annotate_vs(get(obj), Ta_UP_annotated))
}
for (obj in vs_At_names) {
  assign(paste0(obj, "_annotated"), annotate_vs(get(obj), At_UP_annotated))
}

# =============================================================
# 6. Variety ↔ Source mapping
# =============================================================
variety_to_source <- list(
  "FT11" = "FT11",
  "137"  = "FT11",
  "AA"   = "HOR21599",
  "OUN"  = "OUN333",
  "DUL"  = "ZDM01467",
  "HAN"  = "HOR7385",
  "PED"  = "HOR7385",
  "PRO"  = "HOR2180",
  "VAL"  = "HOR2180",
  "OPA"  = "HOR2180",
  "DIA"  = "HOR2180",
  "TRU"  = "HOR2180",
  "ALE"  = "BK",
  "KRO"  = "HOR2180",
  "MOR"  = "Morex",
  "SLA"  = "HOR3081",
  "MIL"  = "MIL",
  "BAR"  = "BK",
  "ACCO" = "RGT",
  "RGT"  = "RGT"
)

# =============================================================
# 7. Helper for merging annotations
# =============================================================
merge_annotation <- function(df, annotation_df, id_col, variety, suffix) {
  match_col <- colnames(annotation_df)[2]
  merged <- merge(df, annotation_df, by.x = id_col, by.y = match_col, all.x = TRUE)
  new_cols <- setdiff(colnames(merged), colnames(df))
  colnames(merged)[colnames(merged) %in% new_cols] <- paste0(variety, "_", suffix, "_", new_cols)
  return(merged)
}

# =============================================================
# 8. Annotate all varieties
# =============================================================
full_ortho_annotated <- full_ortho

for (variety in names(variety_to_source)) {
  source_var <- variety_to_source[[variety]]
  id_col <- grep(paste0("^", variety, "_protein_ID$"), colnames(full_ortho_annotated), value = TRUE)
  if (length(id_col) != 1) {
    warning(paste("⚠️ No unique protein_ID column for", variety))
    next
  }

  annotated_Hv <- get(paste0(source_var, "_vs_Hv_annotated"))
  annotated_Ta <- get(paste0(source_var, "_vs_Ta_annotated"))
  annotated_At <- get(paste0(source_var, "_vs_At_annotated"))

  full_ortho_annotated <- merge_annotation(full_ortho_annotated, annotated_Hv, id_col, variety, "Hv")
  full_ortho_annotated <- merge_annotation(full_ortho_annotated, annotated_Ta, id_col, variety, "Ta")
  full_ortho_annotated <- merge_annotation(full_ortho_annotated, annotated_At, id_col, variety, "At")
}

# =============================================================
# 9. Programmatic reordering
# =============================================================
varieties <- names(variety_to_source)

build_variety_cols <- function(variety, df) {
  base_id <- paste0(variety, "_protein_ID")
  ibaqs   <- grep(paste0("^ibaq_\\d+_", variety, "$"), colnames(df), value = TRUE)
  annots  <- grep(paste0("^", variety, "_"), colnames(df), value = TRUE)
  c(base_id, ibaqs, annots)
}

ordered_cols <- c("Orthogroup", unlist(lapply(varieties, build_variety_cols, df = full_ortho_annotated)))

full_ortho_annotated_reordered <- full_ortho_annotated[, ordered_cols]

# =============================================================
# 10. Save annotated result
# =============================================================
output_path <- "../../../R_Data/Hv4/func_anno2/full_ortho_annotated_postInter.csv"
write.csv(full_ortho_annotated_reordered, output_path, row.names = FALSE)

cat("✅ Annotated file saved:\n", output_path, "\n")



```


#Create candidate gene list for matling enzymes 
In this chunk we create a list of genes for each protein family belonging to the malting enzymes which we are interested in
The method that we use is this one: 
- Take Morex pangenome. Do a BLAST against Morex UniProt reference proteome
- Go in the Morex UniProt reference proteome and find all the proteins we are interested in and get their IDs (ID1). Then go to the MorexUP-MorexPG match and get the corresponding MorexPG IDs (ID2). Then go to MorexUP-BarkePG match and get the corresponding BakePG IDs (ID3)
- In Hv4, we can take all the proteins in the same rows as the ID2 in Manuel Spannagl orthofinder search 
- In Hv5 or Hv6 we can use the Barke IDs (ID3)
- For alpha-amy and other 3/4 families we already have ID1 so we dont have to look for them using name of EC number 
- Last consideration. I noticed that in same cases certain protein found in Morex UniProt have no corresponding protein in Barke. For eg. GLUB2 (Lichenase in MorexUP), cannot be associated to anz protein in Barke. This is why manual curation is necessary. 
- Last last consideration. To best determine which protein in Barke a certain protein in Morex is, we have to run Diamond BLAST. In this waz we can obtain a corresponding list od ID2. But if we do the same thing on MS orthofinder analysis, we will for sure find a sligthly different set of proteins. Depending on the biological Q, I currentlz thing there are two options:
  - Are we interested in comparisons between varieties, we use MorexID (ID2) and we look for corresponding rows in MS orthofinder search. If we were to look for proteins using a Barke protein list we might propagate mismatches
  - Are we interested in Barke only, we use the ID3 file 


```{r}
#In this chunk we create all the files that we can use to build our protein lists 

#Generate a file that contains all the meatches between the full barke proteome and the entries in uniprot and then also contains entries from arabidopsis thaliana uniprot proteome 
#Load the DB matches between Barke and Uniprot barley (full, with all species, more complete)
# set the folder path
path <- "../../../R_import/Hv4/func_anno/Hv_match"

# Load each file separately
barley_hvup <- read.delim(file.path(path, "barke_vs_hvuniprot.tsv"), sep = "\t", header = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
# Keep only first 2 columns and rename them consistently
barley_hvup  <- barley_hvup[, 1:2]
#change col name
colnames(barley_hvup)  <- c("barke_entry", "barleyup_entry")

# Clean annotation files to allow matching 
barley_hvup$barleyup_entry <- sub("^[^|]*\\|[^|]*\\|", "", barley_hvup$barleyup_entry)

#deduplicate entries 
barley_hvup_2 <- barley_hvup %>%
  distinct(barke_entry, .keep_all = TRUE)


##Attach functional annotation from uniprot to the uniprot matched entries 
# set the folder path
path <- "../../../R_import/Hv4/func_anno/masters"

# Load each file separately
up_Hv <- read.delim(file.path(path, "unip_barley_tax_id_4513_2026_01_05.tsv"), sep = "\t", header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Keep only some columns and rename them consistently
up_Hv  <- up_Hv[, c(2,3,4,5,7,6)]
colnames(up_Hv)  <- c("barleyup_entryname", "barleyup_pname", "barleyup_gname", "barleyup_GO_bp", "barleyup_GO_mf", "barleyup_GO_cc")

#Attach the functional annotation to the DB matches and then to the orthology file. I set all.x=TRUE, because i dont want to have annotations for proteins that are not matching 

barley_anno <- merge(
  barley_hvup_2,
  up_Hv,
  by.x = "barleyup_entry",
  by.y = "barleyup_entryname",
  all.x = TRUE
)

#Now associate each barley protein to an arabidopsis protein and attach at uniprot annotations 
# set the folder path
path <- "../../../R_import/Hv6/anno/DB_fasta_vs_upAt"

# Load each file separately
barley_ATup <- read.delim(file.path(path, "araT_barke.tsv"), sep = "\t", header = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Keep only first 2 columns and rename them consistently
barley_ATup  <- barley_ATup[, 1:2]
colnames(barley_ATup)  <- c("barley_entry",  "araT_entry")

# Clean annotation files to allow matching 
barley_ATup$araT_entry <- sub("^[^|]*\\|[^|]*\\|", "", barley_ATup$araT_entry)

#deduplicate entries 
barley_ATup <- barley_ATup %>%
  distinct(barley_entry, .keep_all = TRUE)

#Merge each annotation file with arabidopsis thaliana entries 
barley_anno_expat <- merge(
  barley_anno,
  barley_ATup,
  by.x = "barke_entry",
  by.y = "barley_entry",
  all.x = TRUE
)

#Attach functional annotation from Atuniprot
At_UP_annotated <- read_tsv("../../../R_import/Hv6/anno/masters_up/unip_At_1505.tsv")
At_UP_annotated  <- At_UP_annotated[, c(2,3,4,5,6,7,8)]
At_UP_annotated <- At_UP_annotated %>%
  mutate(TAIR = sub(";.*", "", TAIR))

#Finally get the final annotation file 
barley_full_anno <- merge(
  barley_anno_expat,
  At_UP_annotated,
  by.x = "araT_entry",
  by.y = "Entry Name",
  all.x = TRUE
)
barley_full_anno <- barley_full_anno[, c(2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 1, 14, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13)]
colnames(barley_full_anno) <- c("barley_entry","barleyup_entry","barleyup_pname","barleyup_gname",                               "barleyup_GO_bp","barleyup_GO_mf","barleyup_GO_cc","araT_entry",
                                "TAIR","atup_pname","atup_gname","at_GO_bp",
                                "at_GO_mf","at_GO_cc")


# Save the final annotation file

write.csv(
  barley_full_anno,
  "../../../R_Data/Hv5/func_anno/barley_full_anno_extended.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```


#Here we build the gene lists 
```{r}
#Load our reference (Barke proteome, anntoated with MorexUP and AraTUp)
#It is our ID3, in general good to establish a Barke protein ID list
Barke_ref<-read_csv("../../../R_Data/Hv5/func_anno/barley_full_anno_extended.csv")

#Retrieve Barke protein IDs of candidate protein families as a vectors of comma separated - brackets included entries 
# Beta-glicosidase (EC 3.2.1.21)
bglico <- Barke_ref %>%
  dplyr::filter(grepl("3\\.2\\.1\\.21", .[[3]])) %>%
  dplyr::pull(1)
# Beta-glucanase (EC 3.2.1.4) - Endo 1-4 beta-glucanase
endo_1_4_betagluca <- Barke_ref %>%
  dplyr::filter(grepl("3\\.2\\.1\\.4", .[[3]])) %>%
  dplyr::pull(1)

# Print as a ready-to-paste R character vector
cat(
  paste0(
    "bglico <- c(",
    paste(sprintf('"%s"', bglico), collapse = ", "),
    ")\n"
  )
)
cat(
  paste0(
    "endo_1_4_betagluca <- c(",
    paste(sprintf('"%s"', endo_1_4_betagluca), collapse = ", "),
    ")\n"
  )
)

#Beta-glucanase (Manually curated)
  #Get MorexUP ID
lichenase_ids <- unique(up_Hv[grepl("lichenase", up_Hv[, 2], ignore.case = TRUE) & !is.na(up_Hv[, 2]), 1])
  #From here we manually take the BarkePG ID that have the heighest match with them in the file barley_hvup
lichenase <- c("HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.7HG00679910.1", #GUB2HORVU
               "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.1HG00053050.1" #Q02345_HORVU and Q7DM44_HORVU
               )


```
