```{r}
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.6HG00493590.1" = "chr6H49359",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.6HG00524700.1" = "chr6H52470"
)


# --- 1) Define the family-to-category mapping (in desired order) ---
# ================================================================
# Family → Category mapping (ALIGNED WITH all_families_ID)
# ================================================================

family_category <- c(

  # ======================
  # Amylolysis
  # ======================
  "Alpha-amylase"              = "Amylolysis",
  "Beta-amylase"               = "Amylolysis",
  "Limit dextrinase"           = "Amylolysis",
  "Isoamylase"                 = "Amylolysis",
  "Alpha-glucosidase"          = "Amylolysis",
  "Alpha glucan phosphorylase" = "Amylolysis",

  # ======================
  # Cytolysis (cell wall)
  # ======================
  "Cellulase"              = "Cytolysis",
  "Endo-beta-1,3-glucanase"= "Cytolysis",
  "Beta-glucan exohydrolase" = "Cytolysis",
  "Lichenase"              = "Cytolysis",
  "Xylanase"               = "Cytolysis",
  "Beta-xylosidase"        = "Cytolysis",
  "Arabinofuranosidase"    = "Cytolysis",
  "Mannanase"              = "Cytolysis",
  "Alpha-galactosidase"    = "Cytolysis",
  "Polygalacturonase"      = "Cytolysis",

  # ======================
  # Proteolysis
  # ======================
  "Subtilase"                         = "Proteolysis",
  "Papain-like cysteine protease" = "Proteolysis",
  "Leucine aminopeptidase"          = "Proteolysis",
  "Dipeptidyl-peptidase"            = "Proteolysis",
  "Tripeptidyl-peptidase"           = "Proteolysis",
  "Serine carboxypeptidase"         = "Proteolysis",
  "Serpin"                          = "Proteolysis",

  # ======================
  # Lipid metabolism
  # ======================
  "Lipoxygenase"          = "Lipolysis",
  "Allene oxide synthase" = "Lipolysis"
)

all_families_ID <- readRDS("../../../R_Data/shared_malting_maps/all_families_ID.rds")

# ================================================================
# 1) Collect proteins per enzyme family (based on BAR protein IDs)
# ================================================================
malting_rows <- lapply(names(family_category), function(fam) {
  ids <- all_families_ID[[fam]]
  
  id_cols <- grep("_protein_ID$", names(full_ortho_anno), value = TRUE)
  if (length(id_cols) == 0) stop("❌ No *_protein_ID columns found in full_ortho_anno.")
  
  sub <- full_ortho_anno %>%
    filter(if_any(all_of(id_cols), ~ .x %in% ids))
  
  if (nrow(sub) > 0) {
    sub$enzyme_family <- fam
    sub$Category <- family_category[[fam]]
    return(sub)
  } else {
    return(NULL)
  }
})

malting_all <- bind_rows(malting_rows)
if (nrow(malting_all) == 0) stop("No proteins found for provided all_families_ID list.")
cat("✅ Total proteins collected across all families:", nrow(malting_all), "\n")

# ================================================================
# 2) Extract iBAQ columns & convert to numeric
# ================================================================
ibaq_cols <- grep("^ibaq_", names(malting_all), value = TRUE)

id_cols <- grep("_protein_ID$", names(malting_all), value = TRUE)

ibaq_data <- malting_all %>%
  select(Orthogroup, enzyme_family, Category, all_of(id_cols), all_of(ibaq_cols)) %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("ibaq_"), ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.))))

# Extract BARKE protein ID per row (first matching one)
ibaq_data$Barke_ID <- apply(
  ibaq_data[, id_cols, drop = FALSE],
  1,
  function(x) {
    x <- x[!is.na(x)]
    x[grep("^HORVU.BARKE", x)][1]
  }
)

for (col in ibaq_cols) {
  ibaq_data[[col]][!is.finite(ibaq_data[[col]])] <- 3
}

# ================================================================
# 3) Reorder columns by variety and rename
# ================================================================
variety_order <- c(
  "137","FT11","OUN","AA","DUL","HAN","PED","OPA","PRO",
  "VAL","DIA","TRU","KRO","ALE","MIL","SLA","MOR","BAR","RGT","ACCO"
)

ordered_cols <- unlist(lapply(variety_order, function(v) {
  grep(paste0("_", v, "$"), ibaq_cols, value = TRUE)
}))

ibaq_data <- ibaq_data %>%
  select(Orthogroup, enzyme_family, Category, Barke_ID, all_of(ordered_cols))


time_points <- c("0hs","12hs","24hs","36hs","48hs","60hs","72hs","84hs","96hs")
new_colnames <- unlist(lapply(variety_order, function(v) paste0(v, "_", time_points)))
colnames(ibaq_data)[-(1:4)] <- new_colnames

gene_order_ref <- names(barke_gene_map)
# Add explicit gene order from barke_gene_map
ibaq_data$gene_order <- match(ibaq_data$Barke_ID, gene_order_ref)

# Put unmapped proteins at the end (if any)
ibaq_data$gene_order[is.na(ibaq_data$gene_order)] <- Inf

# ================================================================
# 4) Sort rows  (FIXED)
# ================================================================
category_levels <- c(
  "Amylolysis",
  "Cytolysis",
  "Proteolysis",
  "Lipolysis")

ibaq_data$Category <- factor(
  ibaq_data$Category,
  levels = category_levels
)

ibaq_data$enzyme_family <- factor(
  ibaq_data$enzyme_family,
  levels = names(family_category)
)

ibaq_data <- arrange(ibaq_data, Category, enzyme_family, gene_order)

# ================================================================
# 5) Build numeric matrix (FIXED — exclude gene_order)
# ================================================================

meta_cols <- c("Orthogroup", "enzyme_family", "Category", "Barke_ID", "gene_order")

malting_mat <- as.matrix(ibaq_data[, !(names(ibaq_data) %in% meta_cols)])
mode(malting_mat) <- "numeric"
gene_labels <- barke_gene_map[ibaq_data$Barke_ID]

# Safety fallback: if a BARKE ID is not mapped, show the ID itself
gene_labels[is.na(gene_labels)] <- ibaq_data$Barke_ID[is.na(gene_labels)]

rownames(malting_mat) <- make.unique(gene_labels)

head(ibaq_data[, c("Barke_ID", "gene_order")], 20)

# ================================================================
# 6) Annotation & colors
# ================================================================
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(tidyverse)

annotation_row <- data.frame(
  Family   = ibaq_data$enzyme_family,
  Category = ibaq_data$Category
)
rownames(annotation_row) <- rownames(malting_mat)

family_names <- names(family_category)

family_palette <- adjustcolor(
  colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(12, "Set3"))(length(family_names)),
  alpha.f = 0.9   # softens colours without transparency issues
)

family_colors <- setNames(family_palette, family_names)


category_colors <- c(
  "Amylolysis"    = "#E6C229",  # muted yellow
  "Cytolysis" = "#7FB685",  # soft green
  "Proteolysis"   = "#E07A5F",  # muted coral
  "Lipolysis"     = "#8DA0CB"  # soft blue
)

ann_colors <- list(Family = family_colors, Category = category_colors)
heat_colors <- colorRampPalette(c("#FFFFFF","#FEE8C8","#D73000"))(150)
# ================================================================
# COMPLEX HEATMAPS — GLOBAL SETUP
# ================================================================

library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)
library(grid)

# Nature-safe colour function
library(viridis)

make_col_fun <- function(mat, scale_mode) {

  if (scale_mode == "row") {

    rng <- max(abs(mat), na.rm = TRUE)

    colorRamp2(
      c(-rng, 0, rng),
      c("#FFFFFF", "#FFF7AA", "#D73027")
    )

  } else if (scale_mode == "log2") {

    # ---- FIXED GLOBAL SCALE ----
    colorRamp2(
      c(20, 25, 30),
      c("#FFFFFF", "#FEE8C8", "#D73000")
    )

  } else {

    # ---- RAW (automatic scaling) ----
    qs <- quantile(mat, probs = c(0.01, 0.5, 0.99), na.rm = TRUE)

    colorRamp2(
      qs,
      c("#FFFFFF", "#FEE8C8", "#D73000")
    )
  }
}

#make_col_fun <- function(mat, scale_mode) {
#
 # if (scale_mode == "row") {

  #  rng <- max(abs(mat), na.rm = TRUE)

   # colorRamp2(
    #  c(-rng, 0, rng),
     # c(
      #  viridis(1, begin = 0, end = 0.15),
       # viridis(1, begin = 0.5, end = 0.5),
        #viridis(1, begin = 0.85, end = 1)
      #)
    #)

  #} else {
    # Raw iBAQ: sequential
   # colorRamp2(
    #  quantile(mat, probs = c(0, 0.5, 1), na.rm = TRUE),
    #  viridis(3)
    #)
  #}
#}

# --- FINAL rowname rebuild (CRITICAL FIX) ---
final_labels <- barke_gene_map[ibaq_data$Barke_ID]
final_labels[is.na(final_labels)] <- ibaq_data$Barke_ID[is.na(final_labels)]

rownames(malting_mat) <- make.unique(final_labels)

# ------------------------------------------------
# Core plotting function (REPLACES pheatmap)
# ------------------------------------------------
draw_chm <- function(mat,
                     row_ann,
                     scale_mode = c("row", "log2", "raw"),
                     outfile,
                     width,
                     height,
                     row_font = 6,
                     col_font = 6,
                     title = NULL) {

  scale_mode <- match.arg(scale_mode)

  ext <- tools::file_ext(outfile)

  if (ext == "pdf") {
    grDevices::cairo_pdf(filename = outfile,
                         width = width,
                         height = height,
                         family = "Arial")
  } else if (ext == "png") {
    grDevices::png(outfile,
                   width = width,
                   height = height,
                   units = "in",
                   res = 350)
  } else {
    stop("Unsupported output extension: ", ext)
  }

  on.exit(grDevices::dev.off(), add = TRUE)

  # ---- NOW build everything AFTER device is open ----

  plot_mat <- mat
  if (scale_mode == "row") {
    plot_mat <- t(scale(t(plot_mat)))
    plot_mat[!is.finite(plot_mat)] <- 0
  }

col_fun <- make_col_fun(mat, scale_mode)
  row_ha <- rowAnnotation(
    Family   = row_ann$Family,
    Category = row_ann$Category,
    col = ann_colors,
    annotation_name_gp = gpar(fontsize = 6, fontfamily = "Arial")
  )

  ht <- Heatmap(
    plot_mat,
    name = ifelse(scale_mode == "row", "Z-score", "iBAQ"),
    col = col_fun,
    cluster_rows = FALSE,
    cluster_columns = FALSE,
    show_row_names = TRUE,
    show_column_names = TRUE,
    row_names_side = "left",
    row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = row_font, fontfamily = "Arial"),
    column_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = col_font, fontfamily = "Arial"),
    column_title = title,
    column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 6, fontfamily = "Arial"),
    left_annotation = row_ha,
    rect_gp = gpar(col = "grey60", lwd = 0.4)
  )

  draw(
  ht,
  heatmap_legend_side = "right"
)
}

grDevices::pdf.options(family = "Helvetica")
# ================================================================
# 7) MAIN HEATMAP — ALL FAMILIES
# ================================================================

out_dir <- "../../../R_export/Hv4/malting"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

for (ext in c("pdf", "png")) {

  draw_chm(
    mat = malting_mat,
    row_ann = annotation_row,
    scale_mode = "row",
    outfile = file.path(out_dir, paste0("Hv4_allFamilies_BarkeID.", ext)),
    width = 16,
    height = 12,
    row_font = 5,
    col_font = 5,
    title = "All malting-related enzymes (row-scaled)"
  )
}

# ================================================================
# 8) SUBSET HEATMAPS (GROUPS) — Z-SCORE + RAW
# ================================================================
subset_groups <- list( group1 = c("137","FT11","PED","OUN","DUL","AA"), group2 = c("PED","HAN","PRO"), group3 = c("OPA","VAL","DIA","TRU","KRO","ALE","BAR","RGT"), group4 = c("KRO","ALE","BAR","RGT","ACCO"), group5 = c("MIL","SLA","MOR","BAR") )

group_dims <- list( group1 = c(10,10), group2 = c(7,10), group3 = c(11,10), group4 = c(9,10), group5 = c(9,10) ) 

get_subset_cols <- function(vars, all_cols) { unlist(lapply(vars, function(v) grep(paste0("^",v,"_"), all_cols, value = TRUE))) }

for (g in names(subset_groups)) {

  subset_cols <- get_subset_cols(subset_groups[[g]], colnames(malting_mat))
  sub_raw <- malting_mat[, subset_cols, drop = FALSE]

  keep <- apply(sub_raw, 1, function(x) !all(x %in% c(3, NA)))
  sub_mat <- sub_raw[keep, , drop = FALSE]
  sub_ann <- annotation_row[keep, , drop = FALSE]

  dims <- group_dims[[g]]

  draw_chm(
    mat = sub_mat,
    row_ann = sub_ann,
    scale_mode = "row",
    outfile = file.path(out_dir, paste0("Hv4_allFamilies_", g, ".pdf")),
    width = dims[1],
    height = dims[2],
    row_font = 5,
    col_font = 8,
    title = paste("Group", g, "(row-scaled)")
  )

  draw_chm(
    mat = sub_mat,
    row_ann = sub_ann,
    scale_mode = "raw",
    outfile = file.path(out_dir, paste0("Hv4_allFamilies_", g, "_raw.pdf")),
    width = dims[1],
    height = dims[2],
    row_font = 5,
    col_font = 8,
    title = paste("Group", g, "(raw)")
  )
}
# ================================================================
# Focused dataset — Papain-like cysteine endopeptidase + Beta-amylase
# ================================================================

focus_families <- c(
  "Papain-like cysteine protease",
  "Beta-amylase"
)

focus_data <- ibaq_data %>%
  filter(enzyme_family %in% focus_families)

focus_meta <- c("Orthogroup","enzyme_family","Category","Barke_ID","gene_order")

focus_mat <- as.matrix(focus_data[, !(names(focus_data) %in% focus_meta)])
mode(focus_mat) <- "numeric"

focus_labels <- barke_gene_map[focus_data$Barke_ID]
focus_labels[is.na(focus_labels)] <- focus_data$Barke_ID[is.na(focus_labels)]

rownames(focus_mat) <- make.unique(focus_labels)


focus_annotation <- data.frame(
  Family   = focus_data$enzyme_family,
  Category = focus_data$Category
)

rownames(focus_annotation) <- rownames(focus_mat)



# ================================================================
# 9) FOCUSED HEATMAP — PAPAIN + BETA-AMYLASE
# ================================================================

draw_chm(
  mat = focus_mat,
  row_ann = focus_annotation,
  scale_mode = "row",
  outfile = file.path(out_dir, "Hv4_focus_Papain_BetaAmylase.pdf"),
  width = 14,
  height = 2,
  row_font = 6,
  col_font = 6,
  title = "Papain-like cysteine proteases & β-amylases"
)
# ================================================================
# Collapse to mean iBAQ per variety
# ================================================================

mean_per_variety <- ibaq_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = matches("^(137|FT11|OUN|AA|DUL|HAN|PED|OPA|PRO|VAL|DIA|TRU|KRO|ALE|MIL|SLA|MOR|BAR|RGT|ACCO)_"),
    names_to = "sample",
    values_to = "intensity"
  ) %>%
  mutate(variety = str_extract(sample, "^[A-Z0-9]+")) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup, enzyme_family, Category, variety) %>%
  summarise(mean_ibaq = mean(intensity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = variety, values_from = mean_ibaq) %>%
  arrange(Category, enzyme_family)

mean_meta <- c("Orthogroup","enzyme_family","Category","Barke_ID")

mean_mat <- as.matrix(mean_per_variety[, !(names(mean_per_variety) %in% mean_meta)])
mode(mean_mat) <- "numeric"

mean_per_variety <- mean_per_variety %>%
  left_join(
    distinct(ibaq_data[, c("Orthogroup", "Barke_ID")]),
    by = "Orthogroup"
  )
mean_labels <- barke_gene_map[mean_per_variety$Barke_ID]
mean_labels[is.na(mean_labels)] <- mean_per_variety$Barke_ID[is.na(mean_labels)]

rownames(mean_mat) <- make.unique(mean_labels)


annotation_row_mean <- data.frame(
  Family   = mean_per_variety$enzyme_family,
  Category = mean_per_variety$Category
)

rownames(annotation_row_mean) <- rownames(mean_mat)



# ================================================================
# 10) MEAN PER VARIETY
# ================================================================

draw_chm(
  mat = mean_mat,
  row_ann = annotation_row_mean,
  scale_mode = "row",
  outfile = file.path(out_dir, "Hv4_allFamilies_mean_per_variety.pdf"),
  width = 16,
  height = 12,
  row_font = 3,
  col_font = 5,
  title = "Mean abundance per variety (row-scaled)"
)

# ================================================================
# 11) SINGLE PROTEIN HEATMAP — Bmy1 (LOG2 + RAW)
# ================================================================

target_id <- "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00380960.1"  # Bmy1

# ---- locate row ----
row_idx <- which(ibaq_data$Barke_ID == target_id)

if (length(row_idx) == 0) {
  stop("❌ Target protein not found: ", target_id)
}

if (length(row_idx) > 1) {
  message("⚠️ Multiple entries found for Bmy1 — using first")
  row_idx <- row_idx[1]
}

# ---- extract row (log2 scale) ----
# safety: keep only proper sample columns
# ---- extract row directly from ibaq_data ----
bmy1_log2_vec <- ibaq_data[row_idx, , drop = FALSE]

# ---- keep ONLY valid sample columns (safe) ----
sample_cols <- grep("^[A-Z0-9]+_(0hs|12hs|24hs|36hs|48hs|60hs|72hs|84hs|96hs)$",
                    colnames(bmy1_log2_vec),
                    value = TRUE)

bmy1_log2_vec <- bmy1_log2_vec[, sample_cols, drop = FALSE]
# ------------------------------------------------
# reshape → varieties × time
# ------------------------------------------------
col_info <- data.frame(
  colname = colnames(bmy1_log2_vec)
) %>%
  mutate(
    variety = sub("_.*", "", colname),
    time    = sub(".*_", "", colname)
  )

# ================================================================
# Bmy1 matrix — CLEAN + ROBUST VERSION
# ================================================================

time_levels <- c("0hs","12hs","24hs","36hs","48hs","60hs","72hs","84hs","96hs")

# ------------------------------------------------
# Build long table
# ------------------------------------------------
bmy1_long <- data.frame(
  value   = as.numeric(bmy1_log2_vec[1, ]),
  colname = colnames(bmy1_log2_vec)
) %>%
  mutate(
    variety = sub("_.*", "", colname),
    time    = sub(".*_", "", colname)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(variety), !is.na(time)) %>%
  group_by(variety, time) %>%   # safety aggregation
  summarise(value = mean(value), .groups = "drop")

# ------------------------------------------------
# Build matrix (varieties × time)
# ------------------------------------------------
bmy1_log2_mat <- bmy1_long %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = time, values_from = value)

# ---- enforce correct column order ----
bmy1_log2_mat <- bmy1_log2_mat %>%
  select(variety, any_of(time_levels)) %>%   # safe even if missing cols
  arrange(match(variety, variety_order))

# ---- convert to matrix ----
rownames_vec <- bmy1_log2_mat$variety

bmy1_log2_mat <- bmy1_log2_mat %>%
  select(-variety) %>%
  as.matrix()

rownames(bmy1_log2_mat) <- rownames_vec
mode(bmy1_log2_mat) <- "numeric"

# ------------------------------------------------
# RAW matrix (back-transform from log2)
# ------------------------------------------------
bmy1_raw_mat <- 2^bmy1_log2_mat

# ------------------------------------------------
# Annotation (repeat per variety)
# ------------------------------------------------
bmy1_ann <- data.frame(
  Family   = rep(ibaq_data$enzyme_family[row_idx], nrow(bmy1_log2_mat)),
  Category = rep(ibaq_data$Category[row_idx], nrow(bmy1_log2_mat))
)

rownames(bmy1_ann) <- rownames(bmy1_log2_mat)

# ------------------------------------------------
# Plot — LOG2
# ------------------------------------------------
draw_chm(
  mat = bmy1_log2_mat,
  row_ann = bmy1_ann,
  scale_mode = "log2",
  outfile = file.path(out_dir, "Hv4_Bmy1_allVarieties_log2.pdf"),
  width = 6,
  height = 8,
  row_font = 8,
  col_font = 8,
  title = "Bmy1 (log2 iBAQ)"
)

# ------------------------------------------------
# Plot — RAW
# ------------------------------------------------
draw_chm(
  mat = bmy1_raw_mat,
  row_ann = bmy1_ann,
  scale_mode = "raw",
  outfile = file.path(out_dir, "Hv4_Bmy1_allVarieties_raw.pdf"),
  width = 6,
  height = 8,
  row_font = 8,
  col_font = 8,
  title = "Bmy1 (raw iBAQ)"
)

# ------------------------------------------------
# Plot — Z-score (pattern view)
# ------------------------------------------------
draw_chm(
  mat = bmy1_log2_mat,
  row_ann = bmy1_ann,
  scale_mode = "row",
  outfile = file.path(out_dir, "Hv4_Bmy1_allVarieties_zscore.pdf"),
  width = 6,
  height = 8,
  row_font = 8,
  col_font = 8,
  title = "Bmy1 (row-scaled)"
)
```

###Generate profile plots from candidate protein families 
In this chunk we generate profile plots for candidate protein families. 
Profile plot look better since it gives a temporal resolution 

```{r}

# ===========================================================
# Alpha-amylase expression profiles based on Barke IDs
# ===========================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(ggh4x)

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 1: Load dataset
# -----------------------------------------------------------
full_ortho_anno <- read_csv(
    "../../../R_Data/Hv4/func_anno2/full_ortho_annotated_postInter.csv"
)

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 2: Define Alpha-amylase Barke IDs
# -----------------------------------------------------------
target_barke_IDs <- c(
    "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.3HG00259920.1",
    "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.5HG00441970.1",
    "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.6HG00559240.1",
    "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.6HG00561370.1",
    "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.7HG00653310.1",
    "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.7HG00653330.1"
)

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 3: Extract *_protein_ID columns
# -----------------------------------------------------------
id_cols <- grep("_protein_ID$", colnames(full_ortho_anno), value = TRUE)
if (length(id_cols) == 0) stop("❌ No *_protein_ID columns found.")

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 4: Filter rows matching any Barke ID
# -----------------------------------------------------------
alpha_amylase_data <- full_ortho_anno %>%
    filter(if_any(all_of(id_cols), ~ .x %in% target_barke_IDs))

if (nrow(alpha_amylase_data) == 0) stop("❌ No matching Barke IDs found.")

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 5: Extract iBAQ intensity columns and clean values
# -----------------------------------------------------------
ibaq_cols <- grep("^ibaq_", colnames(alpha_amylase_data), value = TRUE)

ibaq_data <- alpha_amylase_data %>%
    select(all_of(ibaq_cols)) %>%
    mutate(across(everything(), ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.))))

# Convert invalid values → NA → placeholder
ibaq_data[!is.finite(as.matrix(ibaq_data))] <- NA
ibaq_data[is.na(ibaq_data)] <- 0.1  # small placeholder

# Assign Barke ID for facets
ibaq_data$Barke_ID <- make.unique(rep(target_barke_IDs, length.out = nrow(ibaq_data)))

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 6: Long format + group classification
# -----------------------------------------------------------
ibaq_long <- ibaq_data %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -Barke_ID, names_to = "sample", values_to = "iBAQ") %>%
    separate(sample, into = c("prefix", "timepoint", "variety"), sep = "_") %>%
    mutate(
        timepoint   = as.integer(timepoint),
        timepoint_h = factor(paste0((timepoint - 1) * 12, "h"),
                             levels = paste0(seq(0, 96, 12), "h")),
        group = case_when(
            variety %in% c("FT11", "137")               ~ "Ancestors",
            variety %in% c("AA", "OUN", "DUL", "HAN")   ~ "Landraces",
            variety %in% c("ACCO", "RGT")               ~ "Elite Cultivars",
            TRUE                                         ~ "Cultivars"
        ),
        group = factor(group,
                       levels = c("Elite Cultivars", "Cultivars", "Landraces", "Ancestors"))
    )

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 7: Compute mean + SD per group × time × gene
# -----------------------------------------------------------
group_summary <- ibaq_long %>%
    group_by(Barke_ID, group, timepoint_h) %>%
    summarise(
        iBAQ_mean = mean(iBAQ, na.rm = TRUE),
        iBAQ_sd   = sd(iBAQ[iBAQ > 0.1], na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups   = "drop"
    )

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 7b: Specify panel order manually
# -----------------------------------------------------------

custom_order <- c(
    "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.7HG00653310.1",
    "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.3HG00259920.1",
    "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.6HG00559240.1",
    "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.6HG00561370.1",
    "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.7HG00653330.1",
    "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.5HG00441970.1"
)

ibaq_long$Barke_ID     <- factor(ibaq_long$Barke_ID,     levels = custom_order)
group_summary$Barke_ID <- factor(group_summary$Barke_ID, levels = custom_order)

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 8: Plot — lines + ribbons (± 0.5 SD)
# -----------------------------------------------------------
profile_plot <- ggplot() +
    geom_ribbon(
        data = group_summary,
        aes(
            x = timepoint_h,
            ymin = iBAQ_mean - 0.5 * iBAQ_sd,
            ymax = iBAQ_mean + 0.5 * iBAQ_sd,
            fill = group,
            group = group
        ),
        alpha = 0.18
    ) +
    geom_line(
        data = group_summary,
        aes(x = timepoint_h, y = iBAQ_mean, color = group, group = group),
        size = 1.3
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(
        name = "Variety group",
        values = c(
            "Ancestors"       = "#009E73",
            "Landraces"       = "#0072B2",
            "Cultivars"       = "#E69F00",
            "Elite Cultivars" = "#CC79A7"
        )
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(
        name = "Variety group",
        values = c(
            "Ancestors"       = "#009E73",
            "Landraces"       = "#0072B2",
            "Cultivars"       = "#E69F00",
            "Elite Cultivars" = "#CC79A7"
        )
    ) +
    ggh4x::facet_wrap2(~Barke_ID, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3, axes = "all") +
    theme_classic() +
    labs(
        title = "Alpha-amylase expression profiles (Barke IDs)",
        x = "Hours after imbibition",
        y = "log2 Intensity (± 0.5 σ)"
    ) +
    theme(
        axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
        legend.title     = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        legend.text      = element_text(size = 10),
        plot.margin      = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
    )

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 9: Save outputs
# -----------------------------------------------------------
out_dir <- "../../../R_export/Hv4/biased"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

ggsave(
    file.path(out_dir, "Hv4_alpha_amylase_profiles_BarkeID_ordered.jpeg"),
    profile_plot, width = 18, height = 8, dpi = 300, scale = 0.5
)

ggsave(
    file.path(out_dir, "Hv4_alpha_amylase_profiles_BarkeID_ordered.svg"),
    profile_plot, width = 18, height = 8, dpi = 300, scale = 0.5
)

cat("✔ Alpha-amylase profiles (Barke IDs) saved successfully in:", out_dir, "\n")

# ===========================================================
# Alpha-amylase expression profiles based on Barke IDs
# ===========================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(ggh4x)

library(tidyverse)
library(ggh4x)

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 1: Load dataset
# -----------------------------------------------------------
full_ortho_anno <- read_csv(
    "../../../R_Data/Hv4/func_anno2/full_ortho_annotated_postInter.csv"
)

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 2: Define Beta-amylase mapping (ID → short name)
# -----------------------------------------------------------
beta_map <- c(
  "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00380960.1" = "Bmy1",
  "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.2HG00126310.1" = "Bmy2",
  "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.1HG00036700.1" = "Bmy.1",
  "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.1HG00036740.1" = "Bmy.2",
  "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.3HG00228930.1" = "Bmy.3",
  "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00376360.1" = "Bmy.4",
  "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00303690.1" = "Bmy.5",
  "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.1HG00051030.1" = "Bmy.6",
  "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.2HG00126320.1" = "Bmy.7",
  "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.6HG00502080.1" = "Bmy.8",
  "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.2HG00105510.1" = "Bmy.9"
)

target_barke_IDs <- names(beta_map)

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 3: Extract *_protein_ID columns
# -----------------------------------------------------------
id_cols <- grep("_protein_ID$", colnames(full_ortho_anno), value = TRUE)
if (length(id_cols) == 0) stop("❌ No *_protein_ID columns found.")

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 4: Filter rows matching any Barke ID
# -----------------------------------------------------------
beta_data <- full_ortho_anno %>%
    filter(if_any(all_of(id_cols), ~ .x %in% target_barke_IDs))

if (nrow(beta_data) == 0) stop("❌ No matching Barke IDs found.")

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 5: Extract iBAQ columns
# -----------------------------------------------------------
ibaq_cols <- grep("^ibaq_", colnames(beta_data), value = TRUE)

ibaq_data <- beta_data %>%
    select(all_of(ibaq_cols)) %>%
    mutate(across(everything(), ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.))))

ibaq_data[!is.finite(as.matrix(ibaq_data))] <- NA
ibaq_data[is.na(ibaq_data)] <- 0.1

# Assign gene short names instead of IDs
ibaq_data$Gene <- beta_map[
    make.unique(rep(target_barke_IDs, length.out = nrow(ibaq_data)))
]

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 6: Long format
# -----------------------------------------------------------
ibaq_long <- ibaq_data %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -Gene, names_to = "sample", values_to = "iBAQ") %>%
    separate(sample, into = c("prefix", "timepoint", "variety"), sep = "_") %>%
    mutate(
        timepoint   = as.integer(timepoint),
        timepoint_h = factor(paste0((timepoint - 1) * 12, "h"),
                             levels = paste0(seq(0, 96, 12), "h")),
        group = case_when(
            variety %in% c("FT11", "137")             ~ "Ancestors",
            variety %in% c("AA", "OUN", "DUL", "PED") ~ "Landraces",
            variety %in% c( "BAR", "ACCO", "RGT")             ~ "Elite Cultivars",
            TRUE                                       ~ "Cultivars"
        ),
        group = factor(group,
                       levels = c("Elite Cultivars", "Cultivars", "Landraces", "Ancestors"))
    )

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 7: Summary statistics
# -----------------------------------------------------------
group_summary <- ibaq_long %>%
    group_by(Gene, group, timepoint_h) %>%
    summarise(
        iBAQ_mean = mean(iBAQ, na.rm = TRUE),
        iBAQ_sd   = sd(iBAQ[iBAQ > 0.1], na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups   = "drop"
    )

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 8: Set manual order (using short names)
# -----------------------------------------------------------
custom_order <- beta_map[target_barke_IDs]

ibaq_long$Gene     <- factor(ibaq_long$Gene,     levels = custom_order)
group_summary$Gene <- factor(group_summary$Gene, levels = custom_order)

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 9: Plot
# -----------------------------------------------------------
profile_plot <- ggplot() +
    geom_ribbon(
        data = group_summary,
        aes(x = timepoint_h,
            ymin = iBAQ_mean - 0.5 * iBAQ_sd,
            ymax = iBAQ_mean + 0.5 * iBAQ_sd,
            fill = group,
            group = group),
        alpha = 0.18
    ) +
    geom_line(
        data = group_summary,
        aes(x = timepoint_h, y = iBAQ_mean,
            color = group, group = group),
        size = 1
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(
        name = "iBAQ",   # ← legend title changed
        values = c(
            "Ancestors"       = "#009E73",
            "Landraces"       = "#0072B2",
            "Cultivars"       = "#E69F00",
            "Elite Cultivars" = "#CC79A7"
        )
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(
        name = "iBAQ",   # ← legend title changed
        values = c(
            "Ancestors"       = "#009E73",
            "Landraces"       = "#0072B2",
            "Cultivars"       = "#E69F00",
            "Elite Cultivars" = "#CC79A7"
        )
    ) +
    ggh4x::facet_wrap2(~Gene, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2, axes = "all") +
    theme_classic() +
    labs(
        x = "Hours after imbibition",
        y = "iBAQ intensity (± 0.5 σ)"
    ) +
    theme(
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),

    # Remove facet boxes
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 6),

    # ↓↓↓ Make axes thinner
    axis.line        = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.ticks       = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),

    # Optional: shorten tick length
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.15, "cm"),

    legend.title     = element_text(size = 6, face = "bold"),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 6)
)


# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 10: Save
# -----------------------------------------------------------
out_dir <- "../../../R_export/Hv4/biased"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "Hv4_beta_amylase_profiles_shortNames.jpeg"),
       profile_plot, width = 9, height = 6, dpi = 300, scale = 0.6)

ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "Hv4_beta_amylase_profiles_shortNames.svg"),
       profile_plot, width = 9, height = 6, dpi = 300, scale = 0.6)

cat("✔ Beta-amylase profiles (short names) saved successfully in:", out_dir, "\n")

#Beta-amylase

# Step 2: Select orthogroups (beta-amylase)
target_orthogroups <- c("OG0011163", "OG0022946", "OG0015039", "OG0016680", "OG0027012")
all_targets <- full_ortho_anno %>%
  filter(Orthogroup %in% target_orthogroups)

# Step 3: Extract iBAQ and clean
ibaq_data <- all_targets %>%
  select(starts_with("ibaq_")) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.))))
ibaq_data[!is.finite(as.matrix(ibaq_data))] <- NA
ibaq_data[is.na(ibaq_data)] <- 0.1
ibaq_data$Orthogroup <- all_targets$Orthogroup

# Step 4: Reshape
ibaq_long <- ibaq_data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Orthogroup, names_to = "sample", values_to = "iBAQ") %>%
  separate(sample, into = c("prefix", "timepoint", "variety"), sep = "_", remove = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    timepoint   = as.integer(timepoint),
    timepoint_h = factor(paste0((timepoint - 1) * 12, "h"),
                         levels = paste0(seq(0, 96, 12), "h")),
    group = case_when(
      variety %in% c("FT11", "137")             ~ "Ancestors",
      variety %in% c("AA", "OUN", "DUL", "HAN") ~ "Landraces",
      variety %in% c( "BAR", "ACCO", "RGT")  ~ "Elite Cultivars",
      TRUE ~ "Cultivars"
    )
  )

# Step 5: Group summary with mean ± SE
group_summary <- ibaq_long %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup, group, timepoint_h) %>%
  summarise(
    iBAQ_mean = mean(iBAQ, na.rm = TRUE),
    iBAQ_sd   = sd(iBAQ[iBAQ > 0.1], na.rm = TRUE),
    n         = sum(iBAQ > 0.1, na.rm = TRUE),
    iBAQ_se   = ifelse(n > 0, iBAQ_sd / sqrt(n), 0),
    .groups   = "drop"
  )

# Step 6: Plot (no grey lines)
profile_plot <- ggplot() +
  # Ribbons ±SE
  geom_ribbon(data = group_summary,
              aes(x = timepoint_h,
                  ymin = iBAQ_mean - iBAQ_se,
                  ymax = iBAQ_mean + iBAQ_se,
                  fill = group, group = group),
              alpha = 0.25) +
  # Group mean lines
  geom_line(data = group_summary,
            aes(x = timepoint_h, y = iBAQ_mean, color = group, group = group),
            size = 1.3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Ancestors" = "red3",
    "Landraces" = "cornflowerblue",
    "Cultivars" = "forestgreen",
    "Elite Cultivars" = "purple3"
  )) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Ancestors" = "red3",
    "Landraces" = "cornflowerblue",
    "Cultivars" = "forestgreen",
    "Elite Cultivars" = "purple3"
  ), guide = "none") +
  ggh4x::facet_wrap2(~Orthogroup, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3, axes = "all") +
  expand_limits(y = 0) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(title = "Beta-amylase family",
       x = "Time after imbibition", y = "iBAQ Intensity") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

# Step 7: Save
ggsave("../../../R_export/Hv4/biased/Hv4_beta_amylase_profiles.jpeg",
       profile_plot, width = 14, height = 8, dpi = 300, scale=0.6)
```

##Unbiased cluster analysis to see if there are group of proteins that are systematically more abundnat in more modern varieties  

```{r}

# Step 1: Load the full annotated dataset
full_ortho_anno <- read_csv("../../../R_Data/Hv4/func_anno2/full_ortho_annotated.csv")

# Step 2: Extract iBAQ columns and reshape to long format
ibaq_long <- full_ortho_anno %>%
  dplyr::select(Orthogroup, starts_with("ibaq_")) %>%
  pivot_longer(-Orthogroup, names_to = "sample", values_to = "iBAQ") %>%
  mutate(iBAQ = as.numeric(iBAQ))

# Step 3: Parse sample name into metadata (timepoint, variety, group)
ibaq_long <- ibaq_long %>%
  separate(sample, into = c("prefix", "timepoint", "variety"), sep = "_") %>%
  mutate(
    timepoint = as.integer(timepoint),
    timepoint_h = factor(paste0((timepoint - 1) * 12, "h"),
                         levels = paste0(seq(0, 96, by = 12), "h")),
    group = case_when(
      variety %in% c("FT11", "137") ~ "Ancestors",
      variety %in% c("AA", "OUN", "DUL", "HAN") ~ "Landraces",
      variety %in% c("ACCO", "RGT") ~ "Elite",
      TRUE ~ "Cultivars"
    )
  )

# Step 4: Calculate group mean iBAQ per orthogroup and timepoint
group_avg <- ibaq_long %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup, group, timepoint_h) %>%
  mutate(iBAQ = ifelse(is.na(iBAQ), 0.1, iBAQ)) %>%
  summarise(iBAQ = mean(iBAQ, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Step 5: Identify proteins where Elite > all others in ≥7 timepoints
comparison_df <- group_avg %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = group, values_from = iBAQ) %>%
  mutate(
    elite_higher_than_all = Elite > Ancestors + log2(10) &
                            Elite > Landraces + log2(10) &
                            Elite > Cultivars + log2(10)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(elite_high_count = sum(elite_higher_than_all), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(elite_high_count >= 8)

# Step 6: Join back full annotations
dominant_full_anno <- comparison_df %>%
  left_join(full_ortho_anno, by = "Orthogroup")

# Step 7: Get Arabidopsis Entry column (reverse order preference)
at_entry_cols <- grep("_At_Entry$", colnames(dominant_full_anno), value = TRUE)
at_entry_cols_rev <- rev(at_entry_cols)
dominant_full_anno$all_At_Entry <- apply(dominant_full_anno[at_entry_cols_rev], 1, function(row) {
  first_non_na <- row[which(!is.na(row) & row != "")][1]
  if (is.null(first_non_na) || length(first_non_na) == 0) NA else first_non_na
})

# Step 8: Add TAIR IDs
AraT_TAIR <- read_tsv("../../../R_Import/HvGerm_Exp3_RNASeq/AraT_TAIR.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::select(-2) %>%
  mutate(TAIR = sub(";.*$", "", TAIR))

dom_full_anno <- merge(dominant_full_anno, AraT_TAIR,
                       by.x = "all_At_Entry",
                       by.y = "Entry",
                       all.x = TRUE)

# Step 9: Prepare universe
at_entry_cols <- grep("_At_Entry$", colnames(full_ortho_anno), value = TRUE)
at_entry_cols_rev <- rev(at_entry_cols)
full_ortho_anno$all_At_Entry <- apply(full_ortho_anno[at_entry_cols_rev], 1, function(row) {
  first_non_na <- row[which(!is.na(row) & row != "")][1]
  if (is.null(first_non_na) || length(first_non_na) == 0) NA else first_non_na
})

full_ortho_anno_TAIR <- merge(full_ortho_anno, AraT_TAIR,
                              by.x = "all_At_Entry",
                              by.y = "Entry",
                              all.x = TRUE)

universe <- full_ortho_anno_TAIR %>%
  dplyr::select(TAIR) %>%
  filter(!is.na(TAIR)) %>%
  mutate(TAIR = sub(";.*$", "", TAIR))


# ================================
# Step 10 — GO Enrichment Analysis
# ================================


# --- 1️⃣ Perform enrichment analysis
enrich_elite <- enrichGO(
  gene = dom_full_anno$TAIR,
  OrgDb = org.At.tair.db,
  keyType = "TAIR",
  ont = "MF",
  universe = universe$TAIR
)

# --- 2️⃣ Expand multiple gene entries
elite_res <- enrich_elite@result %>%
  separate_rows(geneID, sep = "/")

# ===============================
# Step 11 — Merge with annotations
# ===============================

At_UP_annotated <- read_tsv("../../../R_import/Cam_At_project/Ortho_At_AtUP/UpAt_annotations.tsv")

UpAt_annotations_dist <- At_UP_annotated %>%
  distinct(TAIR, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(TAIR = str_remove(TAIR, ";$"))

elite_res_anno <- merge(
  elite_res,
  UpAt_annotations_dist,
  by.x = "geneID",
  by.y = "TAIR",
  all.x = TRUE
)

# ===================================================
# Step 12 — Filter redundant GO terms by similarity
# ===================================================

# --- 3️⃣ Prepare GO data for semantic similarity (use the same ontology)
sem_data <- godata("org.At.tair.db", ont = "MF", computeIC = TRUE)

# --- 4️⃣ Extract GO term IDs
go_terms <- enrich_elite@result$ID

# --- 5️⃣ Compute pairwise Resnik similarity matrix
sim_matrix <- mgoSim(go_terms, go_terms, semData = sem_data, measure = "Lin", combine = NULL)

# --- 6️⃣ Clean and prepare matrix
sim_matrix[is.na(sim_matrix)] <- 0
diag(sim_matrix) <- 1

# --- 7️⃣ Build adjacency matrix for high similarity (≥ 0.9)
cutoff <- 0.6
adj <- sim_matrix >= cutoff
diag(adj) <- FALSE

# --- 8️⃣ Create a graph and identify redundant clusters
g <- graph_from_adjacency_matrix(adj, mode = "undirected")
clusters <- components(g)$membership

# --- 9️⃣ Keep only one representative GO term per cluster (lowest p.adjust)
selected <- sapply(unique(clusters), function(cl) {
  members <- which(clusters == cl)
  pvals <- enrich_elite@result$p.adjust[members]
  members[which.min(pvals)]
})

# --- 🔟 Extract simplified GO result table
enrich_elite_simplified <- enrich_elite@result[selected, c("ID", "Description", "p.adjust")]
rownames(enrich_elite_simplified) <- NULL


# ===================================================
# Step 13 — Filter enrich_elite to simplified GOs only
# ===================================================

simplified_IDs <- enrich_elite_simplified$ID

# Keep only simplified GO terms in the enrichment object
enrich_elite@result <- enrich_elite@result %>%
  dplyr::filter(ID %in% simplified_IDs)

cat("\n✅ enrich_elite filtered to", nrow(enrich_elite@result), "non-redundant GO terms.\n")
print(enrich_elite@result[, c("ID", "Description", "p.adjust")])




###Now we can do the opposite and check what happpens with the proteins overexpressed in ancestors 

# Step 1: Load the full annotated dataset
full_ortho_anno <- read_csv("../../../R_Data/Hv4/func_anno2/full_ortho_annotated.csv")
# Step 2: Extract iBAQ columns and reshape to long format
ibaq_long <- full_ortho_anno %>%
  dplyr::select(Orthogroup, starts_with("ibaq_")) %>%
  pivot_longer(-Orthogroup, names_to = "sample", values_to = "iBAQ") %>%
  mutate(iBAQ = as.numeric(iBAQ))

# Step 3: Parse sample name into metadata (timepoint, variety, group)
ibaq_long <- ibaq_long %>%
  separate(sample, into = c("prefix", "timepoint", "variety"), sep = "_") %>%
  mutate(
    timepoint = as.integer(timepoint),
    timepoint_h = factor(paste0((timepoint - 1) * 12, "h"),
                         levels = paste0(seq(0, 96, by = 12), "h")),
    group = case_when(
      variety %in% c("FT11", "137") ~ "Ancestors",
      variety %in% c("AA", "OUN", "DUL", "HAN") ~ "Landraces",
      variety %in% c("ACCO", "RGT", "BAR") ~ "Elite",
      TRUE ~ "Cultivars"
    )
  )

# Step 4: Calculate group mean iBAQ per orthogroup and timepoint
group_avg <- ibaq_long %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup, group, timepoint_h) %>%
  mutate(iBAQ = ifelse(is.na(iBAQ), 0.1, iBAQ)) %>%
  summarise(iBAQ = mean(iBAQ, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Step 5: Identify proteins where Ance_Land > Modern in ≥7 timepoints
comparison_df <- group_avg %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = group, values_from = iBAQ) %>%  # Reshape to wide format
  mutate(
    ancestors_higher_than_all = Ancestors > Elite + log2(8) &
                                Ancestors > Landraces + log2(5) &
                                Ancestors > Cultivars + log2(8)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(ancestors_high_count = sum(ancestors_higher_than_all), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(ancestors_high_count >= 7)  # Proteins where Ancestors > all others in ≥7 timepoints

# Step 6: Join back full annotations and iBAQ intensities
dominant_full_anno <- comparison_df %>%
  left_join(full_ortho_anno, by = "Orthogroup")  # Merge with full annotation data to retrieve full info

##Perform an enrichment analysis 

# Step 1: Get the At_Entry columns in reverse order
at_entry_cols <- grep("_At_Entry$", colnames(dominant_full_anno), value = TRUE)
at_entry_cols_rev <- rev(at_entry_cols)  # reverse order

# Step 2: Create the new column by row-wise checking
dominant_full_anno$all_At_Entry <- apply(dominant_full_anno[at_entry_cols_rev], 1, function(row) {
  first_non_na <- row[which(!is.na(row) & row != "")][1]
  if (is.null(first_non_na) || length(first_non_na) == 0) NA else first_non_na
})

#Add TAIR IDs
AraT_TAIR<- read_tsv("../../../R_Import/HvGerm_Exp3_RNASeq/AraT_TAIR.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>% 
  dplyr::select(-2) %>%
  mutate(TAIR = sub(";.*$", "", TAIR))

##Merge the files 
dom_full_anno <- merge(dominant_full_anno, AraT_TAIR, 
                   by.x = "all_At_Entry", 
                   by.y = "Entry", 
                   all.x = TRUE)

##Also add TAIR IDs to full_ortho_anno so we can get the universe 
# Step 1: Identify columns ending with "_At_Entry" in reverse order
at_entry_cols <- grep("_At_Entry$", colnames(full_ortho_anno), value = TRUE)
at_entry_cols_rev <- rev(at_entry_cols)

# Step 2: Create a new column with the first non-NA and non-empty "_At_Entry" value from right to left
full_ortho_anno$all_At_Entry <- apply(full_ortho_anno[at_entry_cols_rev], 1, function(row) {
  first_non_na <- row[which(!is.na(row) & row != "")][1]
  if (is.null(first_non_na) || length(first_non_na) == 0) NA else first_non_na
})
##Merge the files 
full_ortho_anno_TAIR <- merge(full_ortho_anno, AraT_TAIR, 
                   by.x = "all_At_Entry", 
                   by.y = "Entry", 
                   all.x = TRUE)
##Obtain universe
universe<-full_ortho_anno_TAIR %>%
  dplyr::select(TAIR) %>%
  filter(!is.na(TAIR))%>%
  mutate(TAIR = sub(";.*$", "", TAIR))

#Perform enrichment analysis on the Arabidopsis annotations 
enrich_ance <- enrichGO(gene = dom_full_anno$TAIR, OrgDb = org.At.tair.db, keyType = "TAIR",ont= "MF",  universe = universe$TAIR, pAdjustMethod="none", minGSSize = 1, maxGSSize = 50000,   qvalueCutoff = 1)

ance_res <- enrich_ance@result
ance_res2 <- ance_res %>%
  separate_rows(geneID, sep = "/")

At_UP_annotated <- read_tsv("../../../R_import/Cam_At_project/Ortho_At_AtUP/UpAt_annotations.tsv")
UpAt_annotations_dist <- At_UP_annotated%>%
  distinct(TAIR, .keep_all = TRUE)
UpAt_annotations_dist <- UpAt_annotations_dist %>%
  mutate(TAIR = str_remove(TAIR, ";$"))

ance_res_anno <- merge(
  ance_res2,
  UpAt_annotations_dist, 
  by.x  = "geneID",  
  by.y  = "TAIR",
  all.x = TRUE
)
#Filter GOs based on similarity 
# Prepare semantic data for similarity calculation
sem_data <- godata("org.At.tair.db", ont = "MF", computeIC = TRUE)

# Extract GO term IDs
go_terms <- enrich_ance@result$ID

# Compute pairwise Lin similarity matrix
sim_matrix <- mgoSim(go_terms, go_terms, semData = sem_data, measure = "Lin", combine = NULL)

# Clean and prepare the matrix
sim_matrix[is.na(sim_matrix)] <- 0
diag(sim_matrix) <- 1

# Build adjacency matrix for high similarity (≥ 0.9)
cutoff <- 0.9
adj <- sim_matrix >= cutoff
diag(adj) <- FALSE

# Create a graph of highly similar GO terms
g <- graph_from_adjacency_matrix(adj, mode = "undirected")

# Identify connected components (redundant clusters)
clusters <- components(g)$membership

# Keep one representative term per cluster (lowest adjusted p-value)
selected <- sapply(unique(clusters), function(cl) {
    members <- which(clusters == cl)
    pvals <- enrich_ance@result$p.adjust[members]
    members[which.min(pvals)]
})

# Extract simplified GO results
enrich_ance_simplified <- enrich_ance@result[selected, c("ID", "Description", "pvalue")]
rownames(enrich_ance_simplified) <- NULL

# ===================================================
# Step 13 — Filter enrich_ance to simplified GOs only
# ===================================================

simplified_IDs_ance <- enrich_ance_simplified$ID

# Keep only simplified GO terms in the enrichment object
enrich_ance@result <- enrich_ance@result %>%
  dplyr::filter(ID %in% simplified_IDs_ance)


##create an image from this analysis

# Create a named list of enrichment results
enrich_list <- list(
  "Ancestors" = enrich_ance,
  "Elite cultivars" = enrich_elite
)

# Merge enrichment results into a compareClusterResult object
merged <- merge_result(enrich_list)

dotplot(merged)


# Save the dotplot
ggsave("../../../R_export/Hv4/unbiased/enrich_GO2/dotplot_ance_elite.png", width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300, scale=0.8)
ggsave("../../../R_export/Hv4/unbiased/enrich_GO2/dotplot_ance_elite.svg", width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300, scale=0.8)

```

##Generate several important images 


```{r}
# Convert to matrix if needed
heatmap_matrix <- as.matrix(heatmap_data)

 # Extract variety names from column names
variety_names <- unique(str_extract(colnames(heatmap_matrix), "(?<=_)[A-Z0-9]+$"))
variety_names <- sort(na.omit(variety_names))  # Ensure sorted & cleaned

# Filter: mark proteins detected in only 1 time point as NA for that variety
filtered_matrix <- heatmap_matrix

for (variety in variety_names) {
  cols <- grep(paste0("_", variety, "$"), colnames(heatmap_matrix), value = TRUE)
  
  detection_counts <- apply(heatmap_matrix[, cols, drop = FALSE], 1, function(x) sum(!is.na(x)))
  
  # Set entire row to NA if protein is present in only one time point
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(heatmap_matrix))) {
    if (detection_counts[i] == 3) {
      filtered_matrix[i, cols] <- NA
    }
  }
}

# Create presence matrix from filtered_matrix
presence_matrix <- sapply(variety_names, function(variety) {
  cols <- grep(paste0("_", variety, "$"), colnames(filtered_matrix), value = TRUE)
  apply(filtered_matrix[, cols, drop = FALSE], 1, function(x) any(!is.na(x)))
})

# Classify proteins
presence_counts <- rowSums(presence_matrix)
core_proteins <- which(presence_counts == length(variety_names))
cloud_proteins <- which(presence_counts == 1)
shell_proteins <- which(presence_counts > 1 & presence_counts < length(variety_names))

# Count per category per variety
core_counts <- colSums(presence_matrix[core_proteins, , drop = FALSE])
shell_counts <- colSums(presence_matrix[shell_proteins, , drop = FALSE])
cloud_counts <- colSums(presence_matrix[cloud_proteins, , drop = FALSE])

# Combine into tidy format
data_expressed <- bind_rows(
  data.frame(Variety = names(core_counts), Category = "Core", Count = core_counts),
  data.frame(Variety = names(shell_counts), Category = "Shell", Count = shell_counts),
  data.frame(Variety = names(cloud_counts), Category = "Cloud", Count = cloud_counts)
)

# Factor setup
data_expressed$Variety <- factor(data_expressed$Variety, levels = variety_names)
data_expressed$Category <- factor(
  recode(data_expressed$Category,
         "Core" = "Core",
         "Shell" = "Shell",
         "Cloud" = "Cloud"),
  levels = rev(c("Core", "Shell", "Cloud"))
)

# Ensure stacking order
data_expressed <- data_expressed %>%
  arrange(Variety, Category)

# Define color palette
color_palette_expressed <- c("Core" = "grey50", 
                             "Shell" = "#3187bd",
                             "Cloud" = "#f16900")
# Define desired variety order
desired_order <- c("FT11", "137", "AA", "OUN", "DUL", "HAN", "PED", "PRO", "VAL", 
                   "OPA", "DIA", "TRU", "ALE", "KRO", "MOR", "SLA", "MIL", "BAR",  
                   "ACCO", "RGT")

# Apply the desired order to the Variety factor
data_expressed$Variety <- factor(data_expressed$Variety, levels = desired_order)

# Plot as percentage (position = "fill")
p <- ggplot(data_expressed, aes(x = Variety, y = Count, fill = Category)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill", color = "black", size = 0.04) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = color_palette_expressed) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12) 
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "Variety", y = "Percentage", fill = "Category"
  )
# Save the plot
output_path <- "../../../R_export/Hv4/Percentage_Proteomes_Per_Variety.png"
ggsave(output_path, plot = p, width = 12, height = 9, dpi = 400, scale=0.5)
```

#Generate a dendrogram for my varieties 

```{r} 
# --- Newick tree with Kneifl + Morex grouped + offsets ---
tree_text <- "
(
  (FT11:2,137:2):4,
  (
    (
      (Du-Li Huang:2,OUN:2,AA:2):2,
      (Haná:2,
        (Hanna Pedigree:2,
          (Proskovetz Hanna:2,
            (Valtickí:2,
              (Diamant:2,
                (Trumpf:2,
                  (Krona:2,(Alexis:2,Barke:2):2):2
                ):2
              ):2
            ):2
          ):2
        ):10
      ):2
    ):10,
    (
      Slaski:2,
      Golden Promise:3,
      RGT Planet:4,
      Accordine:5,
      Kneifl:6,
      Morex:7
    ):24
  ):4
);
"

# --- Read tree ---
custom_tree <- read.tree(text = tree_text)

# Replace normal spaces with non-breaking spaces (so alignment preserves them)
custom_tree$tip.label <- gsub(" ", "\u00A0", custom_tree$tip.label)

# --- Plotting function ---
plot_hv4_dendrogram <- function() {
  par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2))  # margins
  
  max_depth <- max(node.depth.edgelength(custom_tree))
  
  plot(custom_tree,
       type = "phylogram",
       direction = "rightwards",
       use.edge.length = TRUE,
       cex = 0.8,
       align.tip.label = TRUE,
       no.margin = FALSE,
       lty = 1,
       x.lim = c(0, max_depth * 1.3))  # add 30% space for labels
  
  # --- Timeline ---
  timeline_labels <- c("8000 BC","1000 BC","1850 AD","1900","1950","2000")
  timeline_years  <- c(-8000,-1000,1850,1900,1950,2000)
  
  scaled_years <- (timeline_years - min(timeline_years)) /
                  (max(timeline_years) - min(timeline_years)) * max_depth
  
  axis(side = 1,
       at = scaled_years,
       labels = timeline_labels,
       line = 2,
       lwd = 1,
       cex.axis = 0.6,
       lty = 1)
}

# --- Draw on screen ---
plot_hv4_dendrogram()

# --- Save as SVG ---
svg("../../../R_export/Hv4/dendrogram/hv4_dendrogram.svg", width = 8, height = 10, family = "Helvetica")
par(family = "Helvetica")  # or "sans", "Arial"
plot_hv4_dendrogram()
dev.off()

# --- Save as PDF (best for Illustrator editing) ---
pdf("../../../R_export/Hv4/dendrogram/hv4_dendrogram.pdf", 
    width = 5, height = 7)
plot_hv4_dendrogram()
dev.off()
# --- Save as PNG ---
png("../../../R_export/Hv4/dendrogram/hv4_dendrogram.png", width = 2000, height = 2000, res = 300)
plot_hv4_dendrogram()
dev.off()




```

#Correlation proteomic data and genomic data 
In this chunk we attempt to correlate proteomic values with genomic information

```{r}

###Generate a file that contains both all the orthogroup proteins and the identified proteins

# ================
# 1) Load files
# ================
full_ortho_anno <- read_csv("../../../R_Data/Hv4/func_anno2/full_ortho_annotated.csv")
OrthoG_exp <- fread("../../../R_Data/Hv4/Ortho/OrthoG_full.csv")

# ================
# 2) Rename original columns
# ================
OrthoG_exp_all <- OrthoG_exp %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_all_proteins"), -Orthogroup)

# ===========================
# 3) Merge: keep all orthogroups
# ===========================
final_with_all <- OrthoG_exp_all %>%
  left_join(full_ortho_anno, by = "Orthogroup")

# ============================
# 4) Reorder columns by manual index list
# ============================
col_order <- c(
  1, 
  3, 14:51, 
  10, 128:165, 
  12, 166:203,
  9, 546:583,
  4, 622:659,
  2, 660:697,
  11, 736:773
)

# reorder
final_with_all <- final_with_all[, ..col_order]

# ============================
# 5) Save
# ============================
out_dir <- "../../../R_Data/Hv4/genomics_proteomics"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

fwrite(final_with_all, file.path(out_dir, "full_ortho_with_allproteins.csv"))
message("✅ Saved merged table with all proteins, observed IDs, intensities — columns reordered manually")


###Using the file generated test copy number variation

# Load table
full_ortho_with_allproteins <- fread("../../../R_Data/Hv4/genomics_proteomics/full_ortho_with_allproteins.csv")

# Output folder
out_dir <- "../../../R_export/Hv4/genomics_proteomics"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# =========================
# Mapping: short iBAQ code -> full variety name
# =========================
variety_map <- tribble(
  ~ibaq_name, ~variety,
  "FT11",     "FT11_B1K-04-12",
  "OUN",      "OUN333",
  "DUL",      "ZDM01467",
  "MOR",      "Morex",
  "MIL",      "Golden_Promise",
  "BAR",      "Barke",
  "RGT",      "RGT_Planet.primary"
)

# =========================
# 1) COPY NUMBERS from *_all_proteins
# =========================

count_entries <- function(x) {
  ifelse(
    is.na(x) | x == "",
    NA_integer_,
    vapply(strsplit(as.character(x), ","), function(v) {
      sum(nzchar(trimws(v)))
    }, integer(1))
  )
}

cn_long <- full_ortho_with_allproteins %>%
  select(Orthogroup, matches("_all_proteins$")) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Orthogroup,
    names_to = "colname",
    values_to = "ids"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Extract short variety code (before first underscore)
    ibaq_name = str_extract(colname, "^[A-Za-z0-9]+"),
    copy_count = count_entries(ids)
  ) %>%
  left_join(variety_map, by = "ibaq_name") %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup, variety) %>%
  summarise(copy_number = sum(copy_count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

cat("CN check: rows =", nrow(cn_long), "\n")
print(head(cn_long, 5))
print(cn_long %>% count(variety))

# =========================
# 2) iBAQ data (all timepoints)
# =========================

ibaq_long <- full_ortho_with_allproteins %>%
  select(Orthogroup, starts_with("ibaq_")) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Orthogroup,
    names_to = "sample",
    values_to = "iBAQ"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    timepoint = as.integer(str_extract(sample, "(?<=^ibaq_)\\d+")),
    ibaq_name = sub(".*_([^_]+)$", "\\1", sample)
  ) %>%
  left_join(variety_map, by = "ibaq_name") %>%
  mutate(
    timepoint_h = factor(paste0((timepoint - 1) * 12, "h"),
                         levels = paste0(seq(0, 96, by = 12), "h")),
    class = case_when(
      variety %in% c("FT11_B1K-04-12")                 ~ "Ancestors",
      variety %in% c("OUN333", "ZDM01467")             ~ "Landraces",
      variety %in% c("RGT_Planet.primary")             ~ "Elite Cultivars",
      variety %in% c("Morex", "Golden_Promise", "Barke") ~ "Cultivars",
      TRUE                                              ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

cat("iBAQ long check: rows =", nrow(ibaq_long), "\n")
print(ibaq_long %>% count(variety, class))

# =========================
# 3) Average iBAQ per (Orthogroup, variety)
# =========================

ibaq_avg <- ibaq_long %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup, variety) %>%
  summarise(intensity = mean(iBAQ, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

cat("iBAQ avg check: non-NA intensities =",
    sum(!is.na(ibaq_avg$intensity)), "/", nrow(ibaq_avg), "\n")

# =========================
# 4) Merge CN + iBAQ
# =========================

merged <- cn_long %>%
  left_join(ibaq_avg, by = c("Orthogroup", "variety")) %>%
  left_join(ibaq_long %>% distinct(variety, class), by = "variety")

cat("Merged check: rows =", nrow(merged), "\n")
print(head(merged, 8))

# =========================
# 5) CORRELATION between CN and intensity
# =========================

corr_data <- merged %>%
  filter(!is.na(copy_number), !is.na(intensity),
         intensity >= 3, copy_number <= 50)

cat("corr_data rows =", nrow(corr_data), "\n")
print(corr_data %>% count(class))

if (nrow(corr_data) > 2) {
  cor_res <- cor.test(corr_data$copy_number, corr_data$intensity, method = "spearman")
  rho_val <- round(cor_res$estimate, 3)
  p_val <- signif(cor_res$p.value, 3)
  print(cor_res)

  p_corr <- ggplot(corr_data, aes(x = copy_number, y = intensity, color = class)) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.6, size = 1.3) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.2))) +
    theme_classic() +
    labs(
      title = "Correlation between Copy Number (from *_all_proteins) and Protein Intensity",
      x = "Copy number (per variety, per orthogroup)",
      y = "Mean iBAQ Intensity (log2 scale)",
      color = "Class"
    ) +
    annotate("text",
             x = max(corr_data$copy_number, na.rm = TRUE) + 3,
             y = min(corr_data$intensity, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.2,
             label = paste0("Spearman rho = ", rho_val, "\nP = ", p_val),
             hjust = 0, vjust = 0, size = 2)

  ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "correlation_copyNumber_vs_intensity_allProteins.png"),
         p_corr, width = 10, height = 4, scale = 0.8)
} else {
  message("Not enough points for correlation (n <= 2). Check mapping or filters.")
}

# =====================
# 6) ELITE vs ANCESTORS COMPARISON
# =====================

group_avg <- ibaq_long %>%
  filter(class %in% c("Elite Cultivars", "Ancestors")) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  filter(all(iBAQ >= 0.1 | is.na(iBAQ))) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup, class, timepoint_h) %>%
  mutate(iBAQ = ifelse(is.na(iBAQ), 0.1, iBAQ)) %>%
  summarise(iBAQ = mean(iBAQ, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

comparison_df <- group_avg %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = class, values_from = iBAQ) %>%
  mutate(
    elite_higher_than_anc = `Elite Cultivars` > (`Ancestors` + log2(30))
  ) %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup) %>%
  summarise(elite_high_count = sum(elite_higher_than_anc, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(elite_high_count >= 8)

sig_elite <- comparison_df$Orthogroup
cat("Number of proteins consistently higher in elite vs ancestors:", length(sig_elite), "\n")

# =====================
# 7) BINOMIAL CN COMPARISON
# =====================

copy_compare <- merged %>%
  filter(Orthogroup %in% sig_elite,
         class %in% c("Elite Cultivars", "Ancestors")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(copy_number)) %>%
  select(Orthogroup, class, copy_number)

cn_wide <- copy_compare %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = class, values_from = copy_number) %>%
  filter(!is.na(`Elite Cultivars`) & !is.na(Ancestors)) %>%
  mutate(elite_gt_anc = ifelse(`Elite Cultivars` > Ancestors, 1, 0))

successes <- sum(cn_wide$elite_gt_anc, na.rm = TRUE)
n_total   <- nrow(cn_wide)
cat("Elite>Ancestor CN successes / total:", successes, "/", n_total, "\n")

if (n_total > 0) {
  binom_res <- binom.test(successes, n_total, p = 0.5, alternative = "greater")
  print(binom_res)
} else {
  message("No orthogroups passed the elite-vs-ancestor filter; binomial test skipped.")
}

# =====================
# 8) PLOTS
# =====================

if (nrow(copy_compare) > 0) {
  p_cn_dist <- ggplot(copy_compare, aes(x = class, y = copy_number, fill = class)) +
    geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, outlier.shape = NA) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    labs(title = "Copy number distribution: Elite vs Ancestors (from *_all_proteins)",
         x = "Class", y = "Copy number")

  ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "copy_number_distribution_elite_vs_ancestors_allProteins.png"),
         p_cn_dist, width = 6, height = 5)

  frac_df <- tibble(
    category = c("Observed", "Expected"),
    fraction = c(ifelse(n_total > 0, successes / n_total, NA_real_), 0.5)
  )

  p_frac <- ggplot(frac_df, aes(x = category, y = fraction, fill = category)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.5) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed") +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    labs(title = "Fraction of proteins with Elite CN > Ancestor CN (*_all_proteins)",
         y = "Fraction", x = "")

  ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "fraction_elite_gt_ancestor_allProteins.png"),
         p_frac, width = 5, height = 4)
} else {
  message("No data to plot CN distributions.")
}

cat("✔ All *_all_proteins correlation and comparison steps completed successfully.\n")

## -----------------------------------------------------------
## Generate copy-number & averaged-intensity matrices for orthogroups
## -----------------------------------------------------------

# -------------------------------
# 0) Helper functions
# -------------------------------

count_entries <- function(x) {
  ifelse(
    is.na(x) | x == "",
    0L,
    vapply(strsplit(as.character(x), ","), function(v) {
      sum(nzchar(trimws(v)))
    }, integer(1))
  )
}

# Build Orthogroup → enzyme family lookup
og_to_family <- tibble(
  Orthogroup    = unlist(all_families, use.names = FALSE),
  enzyme_family = rep(names(all_families), lengths(all_families))
)

# Create output dir
out_dir <- "../../../R_export/Hv4"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# -------------------------------
# 1) Orthogroups of interest
# -------------------------------
og_interest <- unique(unlist(all_families, use.names = FALSE))
cat("Number of orthogroups of interest:", length(og_interest), "\n")

# -------------------------------
# 2) COPY-NUMBER MATRIX
# -------------------------------
all_protein_cols <- grep("_all_proteins$", colnames(full_ortho_with_allproteins), value = TRUE)
cat("Detected", length(all_protein_cols), "all_protein columns\n")

copy_number_matrix <- full_ortho_with_allproteins %>%
  filter(Orthogroup %in% og_interest) %>%
  select(Orthogroup, all_of(all_protein_cols)) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(all_protein_cols), count_entries)) %>%
  left_join(og_to_family, by = "Orthogroup") %>%
  relocate(enzyme_family, .after = Orthogroup)

# Remove rows with no variation
copy_number_matrix <- copy_number_matrix %>%
  filter(apply(select(., all_of(all_protein_cols)), 1, function(x) {
    ux <- unique(x)
    ux <- ux[is.finite(ux)]
    length(ux) > 1
  }))

# Save copy-number matrix
out_copy <- file.path(out_dir, "protein_copy_number_matrix.csv")
write.csv(copy_number_matrix, out_copy, row.names = FALSE)
cat("✔ Copy-number matrix saved to:", out_copy, "\n")

# Retain orthogroups that remain after filtering
ogs_to_keep <- unique(copy_number_matrix$Orthogroup)
cat("→ Retaining", length(ogs_to_keep), "orthogroups for intensity analysis\n")

# -------------------------------
# 3) INTENSITY MATRIX (average per variety)
# -------------------------------
ibaq_cols <- grep("^ibaq_[0-9]+_", colnames(full_ortho_with_allproteins), value = TRUE)
cat("Detected", length(ibaq_cols), "iBAQ columns\n")

intensity_subset <- full_ortho_with_allproteins %>%
  filter(Orthogroup %in% ogs_to_keep) %>%
  select(Orthogroup, all_of(ibaq_cols)) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(ibaq_cols), ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.))))

# Convert to long and extract variety codes
intensity_long <- intensity_subset %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(ibaq_cols), names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Intensity") %>%
  mutate(Variety = str_extract(Sample, "[A-Z0-9]+$"))

# Average per (Orthogroup, Variety)
intensity_wide <- intensity_long %>%
  group_by(Orthogroup, Variety) %>%
  summarise(mean_intensity = mean(Intensity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Variety, values_from = mean_intensity) %>%
  left_join(og_to_family, by = "Orthogroup") %>%
  relocate(enzyme_family, .after = Orthogroup)

# Determine which varieties are actually present
desired_order <- c("FT11","137","AA","OUN","DUL","HAN","PED","PRO","VAL",
                   "OPA","DIA","TRU","KRO","ALE","MOR","SLA","MIL","BAR","ACCO","RGT")
present_varieties <- intersect(desired_order, colnames(intensity_wide))

# Reorder columns
intensity_wide <- intensity_wide %>%
  select(Orthogroup, enzyme_family, all_of(present_varieties))

# Remove rows with no variation
intensity_wide <- intensity_wide %>%
  filter(apply(select(., all_of(present_varieties)), 1, function(x) {
    x <- as.numeric(x)
    x <- x[is.finite(x)]
    length(unique(x)) > 1
  }))

# Save intensity matrix
out_intensity <- file.path(out_dir, "protein_intensity_matrix.csv")
write.csv(intensity_wide, out_intensity, row.names = FALSE)
cat("✔ Intensity matrix saved to:", out_intensity, "\n")

# -------------------------------
# 4) Summary
# -------------------------------
cat("✔ Both matrices successfully generated.\n")
cat("   → Copy number: ", out_copy, "\n")
cat("   → Intensities: ", out_intensity, "\n")
cat("   → Orthogroups retained for both:", length(ogs_to_keep), "\n")




```

#Investigation of beta-amylase peptide abundance 

```{r}
# =============================================================
# PEPTIDE PIPELINE — Multi-batch, adaptive correction, TSN, smoothing,
# with z-scored and non-z-scored heatmaps per variety (unique peptides only + PDF export)
# =============================================================

library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(pheatmap)
library(stringr)

# ---------------------- USER PARAMETERS ----------------------
pept_dir   <- "../../../R_import/Hv4/MW_pipe/peptides"
out_base   <- "../../../R_export/Hv4/peptides_HOG0042218"
dir.create(normalizePath(out_base, mustWork = FALSE), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

ortho_file <- "../../../R_Data/Hv4/single_inter/Hv4_allBatches_interBatchNorm.csv"
target_hog <- "N1.HOG0042218"
min_val       <- 0.1
missing_limit <- 9
smooth_k      <- 3

# =============================================================
# STEP 1 — Load ortholog mapping for the target HOG
# =============================================================
message("📂 Loading ortholog mapping from: ", ortho_file)
full_ortho <- fread(ortho_file)

id_cols <- grep("_protein_ID$", names(full_ortho), value = TRUE)
subset_dt <- full_ortho[, c("HOG", id_cols), with = FALSE]
result <- subset_dt[HOG == target_hog]
if (nrow(result) == 0) stop("❌ Target HOG not found in ortholog table.")
message("✅ Found entry for ", target_hog)

# =============================================================
# STEP 2 — Define file–variety–protein mapping
# =============================================================
file_variety_map <- data.table(
    File = c(
        "peptides_b1", "peptides_b2", "peptides_b3", "peptides_b4", "peptides_b5",
        "peptides_b6", "peptides_b7", "peptides_b8", "peptides_b9", "peptides_b10",
        "peptides_b11", "peptides_b12", "peptides_b13", "peptides_b14", "peptides_b15",
        "peptides_b16", "peptides_b17", "peptides_b18", "peptides_b19", "peptides_b20"
    ),
    Variety = c(
        "RGT","ACCO","SLA","OUN","AA","DUL","VAL","MIL","137","PED",
        "BAR","PRO","DIA","MOR","TRU","FT11","OPA","ALE","KRO","HAN"
    )
)

protein_cols <- names(result)[grepl("_protein_ID$", names(result))]
variety_from_result <- sub("_protein_ID$", "", protein_cols)
variety_protein_map <- data.table(
    Variety = variety_from_result,
    Protein_ID = as.character(result[1, ..protein_cols])
)

variety_map_full <- merge(file_variety_map, variety_protein_map, by = "Variety", all.x = TRUE)
qc_path <- file.path(out_base, "QC_file_variety_protein_map.csv")
fwrite(variety_map_full, qc_path)
message("✅ Variety-to-protein mapping saved to ", qc_path)

file2batch  <- setNames(variety_map_full$Variety, gsub("^peptides_", "", variety_map_full$File))
variety2prot <- setNames(variety_map_full$Protein_ID, variety_map_full$Variety)

# =============================================================
# STEP 3 — Processing configuration
# =============================================================
batch_thresholds <- list(
    "RGT"=2,"ACCO"=2,"AA"=2,"DUL"=1.8,"VAL"=2,"MIL"=2,"PED"=1.8,"PRO"=2,
    "DIA"=3.5,"MOR"=1.6,"ALE"=2,"KRO"=1.8,"HAN"=1.8,"SLA"=3,"OUN"=2,
    "BAR"=1.8,"TRU"=2,"FT11"=2.5,"OPA"=1.8,"137"=1.6
)

batch_specific_rules <- list(
    "ACCO"=list(list(target=5, neighbors=c(4,6))),
    "TRU" =list(list(target=3, neighbors=c(2,4)),
                list(target=5, neighbors=c(4,6)),
                list(target=8, neighbors=c(7,9))),
    "RGT" =list(list(target=1, neighbors=c(2))),
    "PRO" =list(list(target=3, neighbors=c(2,4))),
    "DUL" =list(list(target=8, neighbors=c(7,9))),
    "MOR" =list(list(target=8, neighbors=c(7,9))),
    "PED" =list(list(target=4, neighbors=c(3)),
                list(target=6, neighbors=c(7)),
                list(target=5, neighbors=c(7))),
    "OPA" =list(list(target=3, neighbors=c(2)),
                list(target=5, neighbors=c(6))),
    "137" =list(list(target=6, neighbors=c(7)),
                list(target=4, neighbors=c(3)),
                list(target=5, neighbors=c(7))),
    "HAN" =list(list(target=3, neighbors=c(2)),
                list(target=4, neighbors=c(2)),
                list(target=5, neighbors=c(6))),
    "DIA" =list(list(target=2, neighbors=c(1,3))),
    "KRO" =list(list(target=4, neighbors=c(3,6)),
                list(target=5, neighbors=c(3,6)))
)

batches_1to9  <- c("b1","b3","b5","b7","b9","b11","b13","b15","b17","b19")
batches_10to18 <- c("b2","b4","b6","b8","b10","b12","b14","b16","b18","b20")

# =============================================================
# STEP 4 — Helper functions
# =============================================================
san <- function(x) gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9._-]+", "_", x)
edge_ma <- function(x, k=3){
    if (all(is.na(x))) return(x)
    h <- floor(k/2)
    x_ext <- c(rep(x[1], h), x, rep(x[length(x)], h))
    y <- stats::filter(x_ext, rep(1/k, k), sides=2)
    as.numeric(y[(h+1):(length(y)-h)])
}
smooth_matrix <- function(mat, k=3) t(apply(mat, 1, edge_ma, k=k))
select_batch_channels <- function(dt, stem) {
    if (stem %in% batches_1to9) {
        message("📥 Selecting columns: Reporter.intensity.corrected.[1–9]")
        cols <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.[1-9]", names(dt), value = TRUE)
    } else if (stem %in% batches_10to18) {
        message("📥 Selecting columns: Reporter.intensity.corrected.1[0–8]")
        cols <- grep("Reporter.intensity.corrected.1[0-8]", names(dt), value = TRUE)
    } else stop("❌ Unknown batch: ", stem)
    cols[1:min(length(cols),9)]
}

# =============================================================
# STEP 5 — Main processing loop
# =============================================================

pept_files <- list.files(pept_dir, pattern="^peptides_b.*\\.txt$", full.names=TRUE)
message("Detected ", length(pept_files), " peptide files.")
if (!length(pept_files)) stop("❌ No peptide files found at ", normalizePath(pept_dir))

# store normalized batches for later inter-batch normalization
norm_batches <- list()

for (fp in pept_files) {

    message("\n--------------------------------")
    message("📄 File: ", basename(fp))

    dt <- fread(fp, sep="\t", header=TRUE, quote="", data.table=TRUE)
    if (!"Proteins" %in% names(dt)) {
        message("⚠️ No 'Proteins' column — skipped.")
        next
    }

    stem  <- tolower(gsub("^peptides_|\\.txt$", "", basename(fp)))
    batch <- file2batch[[stem]]

    if (is.null(batch)) {
        message("⚠️ No variety mapping for ", stem)
        next
    }

    var_id <- variety2prot[[batch]]
    if (is.null(var_id) || is.na(var_id)) {
        message("⚠️ Missing protein ID for ", batch)
        next
    }

    thr   <- batch_thresholds[[batch]]
    if (is.null(thr)) thr <- 2

    rules <- batch_specific_rules[[batch]]

    message("🔍 Variety: ", batch, " → Protein ID: ", var_id)

    chans_raw <- select_batch_channels(dt, stem)
    setnames(dt, chans_raw, paste0("Ch", seq_along(chans_raw)))
    chans <- paste0("Ch", seq_along(chans_raw))

    keep <- apply(dt[, ..chans], 1, function(x)
        sum(is.na(x) | x < min_val) < missing_limit)

    dt <- dt[keep]

    if (!nrow(dt)) next

    dt[, ProteinCount := lengths(strsplit(Proteins, ";"))]
    dt <- dt[ProteinCount == 1]
    dt[, ProteinCount := NULL]

    if (!nrow(dt)) {
        message("⚠️ No unique peptides found — skipping.")
        next
    }

    mat <- as.matrix(dt[, ..chans])
    mode(mat) <- "numeric"

    n_imp <- 0
    n_rep <- 0

    for (i in seq_len(nrow(mat))) {

        row <- mat[i,]

        if (!is.null(rules)) {

            for (r in rules) {

                t <- r$target
                neigh <- row[r$neighbors]
                neigh <- neigh[is.finite(neigh) & neigh > 0]

                if (length(neigh)) {

                    mu <- mean(neigh)
                    val <- row[t]

                    if (is.na(val) || val < min_val) {

                        row[t] <- mu
                        n_imp <- n_imp + 1

                    } else if (abs(log2((val+1)/(mu+1))) > thr) {

                        row[t] <- mu
                        n_rep <- n_rep + 1
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        target_idx <- if (is.null(rules)) integer(0)
        else vapply(rules, `[[`, numeric(1), "target")

        for (j in seq_along(row)) {

            if (j %in% target_idx) next

            neigh <- row[intersect(c(j-1,j+1), seq_along(row))]
            neigh <- neigh[is.finite(neigh) & neigh > 0]

            if (!length(neigh)) next

            mu <- mean(neigh)
            val <- row[j]

            if (is.na(val) || val < min_val) {

                row[j] <- mu
                n_imp <- n_imp + 1

            } else if (abs(log2((val+1)/(mu+1))) > thr) {

                row[j] <- mu
                n_rep <- n_rep + 1
            }
        }

        mat[i,] <- row
    }

    message(sprintf("✅ %s — Corrected: %d (imputed %d, replaced %d)",
        batch, n_imp+n_rep, n_imp, n_rep))

    # -----------------------------
    # Intra-batch TSN
    # -----------------------------

    col_sums <- colSums(mat, na.rm=TRUE)
    gm <- median(col_sums, na.rm=TRUE)
    fac <- gm / col_sums

    for (j in seq_along(chans))
        mat[,j] <- mat[,j] * fac[j]

    # -----------------------------
    # Smoothing
    # -----------------------------

    mat <- smooth_matrix(mat, k=smooth_k)

    hit <- grepl(var_id, dt$Proteins, fixed=TRUE)
    if (!any(hit)) next

    sub_mat <- log2(mat[hit,,drop=FALSE] + 1)

    rownames(sub_mat) <- make.unique(dt$Sequence[hit])

    meta_cols <- intersect(
        c("Sequence","Proteins","Leading razor protein",
          "Start position","End position"),
        names(dt))

    sub_meta <- dt[hit, ..meta_cols]

    out_tab <- cbind(sub_meta, as.data.table(sub_mat))

    # store batch table
    norm_batches[[batch]] <- out_tab
}

message("\n🎯 Completed per-batch normalization.")


# =============================================================
# STEP 6 — INTER-BATCH MEDIAN CENTERING
# =============================================================

cat("\n--- Performing inter-batch median centering ---\n")

batch_names <- names(norm_batches)

batch_medians <- sapply(batch_names, function(v) {

    dt <- norm_batches[[v]]

    intensity_cols <- grep("^V[0-9]+$", names(dt), value=TRUE)

    vals <- unlist(dt[, ..intensity_cols])
    vals <- vals[is.finite(vals)]

    median(vals, na.rm=TRUE)
})

grand_median <- median(batch_medians, na.rm=TRUE)
cor_factors  <- grand_median / batch_medians

print(cor_factors)

for (v in batch_names) {

    f <- cor_factors[v]

    dt <- norm_batches[[v]]

    intensity_cols <- grep("^V[0-9]+$", names(dt), value=TRUE)

    dt[, (intensity_cols) :=
        lapply(.SD, function(x) x * f),
        .SDcols=intensity_cols]

    norm_batches[[v]] <- dt
}

cat("✅ Inter-batch normalization complete.\n")


# =============================================================
# STEP 7 — EXPORT FINAL TABLES
# =============================================================

for (v in batch_names) {

    dt <- norm_batches[[v]]
```
